name: freee MCP
description: Cloud accounting, invoicing, HR, payroll, and time tracking for Japanese businesses. 5 business domains in one MCP server. OAuth 2.0+PKCE. Remote MCP available.

guide.auth_overview: OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code + PKCE. Register app at freee Developer Portal. Exchange auth code for access_token (expires 24h). Use refresh_token (expires 90 days) to renew. MCP server handles auth automatically via npx @freee/mcp-server.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Register at app.secure.freee.co.jp/developers. 2. Create app, set redirect_uri. 3. Get company_id from GET /companies. 4. Most endpoints require company_id param. 5. Or just use: npx @freee/mcp-server (handles OAuth automatically).

endpoints_sample.description: Create a new deal (invoice, expense, etc.)

agent_tips_sample: Almost every endpoint requires company_id — always call GET /companies first to get it.
Amount fields are integers in JPY (no decimal). 1000 = ¥1,000.
Date format: YYYY-MM-DD. All dates are JST (UTC+9).
guide.rate_limit: 3,600 requests/hour per access token. Wallets/deals endpoints: 300/5min. HTTP 429 with rate-limit headers.

name: Money Forward Cloud MCP
description: Cloud accounting with AI-powered categorization. Journal entry, ledger search, trial balance, report creation. Remote MCP available to all plans since March 2026.

guide.auth_overview: OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code flow. Register app at MF Developer Portal. Or use official MCP server: npx @moneyforward/mcp-server (handles auth).
guide.setup_hint: Easiest: npx @moneyforward/mcp-server (Remote MCP available to all plans since March 2026). Manual: register at developer.moneyforward.com, get client_id/secret.

endpoints_sample.description: List offices (事業所) — get office_id first

agent_tips_sample: office_id is required for most endpoints — always call GET /offices first (same pattern as freee's company_id).
Journal entries (仕訳): debit/credit sides must balance. Use deal_categories to get valid account item IDs.
AI categorization: POST /auto_journal_entries for automatic ML-powered expense categorization.
guide.rate_limit: 300 requests/5 minutes per token. HTTP 429 with Retry-After.

name: Yayoi (弥生会計)
description: Long-established Japanese accounting software. YAYOI SMART CONNECT for bank/card data import. Subsidiary Misoca handles invoice API. Limited public API.

guide.auth_overview: OAuth 2.0 via Yayoi ID. Limited API availability — primarily Yayoi Smart Connect for data import/export.
guide.setup_hint: 1. 弥生IDでログイン. 2. スマート取引取込APIのパートナー申請. 3. OAuth認証フローでaccess_token取得. 4. 注意: APIは限定的で、パートナー申請が必要.
agent_tips_sample: API利用にはパートナー申請が必要 — 個人開発者には開放されていない.
スマート取引取込がメインのAPI — 他の機能のAPIは限定的.
弥生会計/弥生青色申告のデータは直接APIアクセス不可 — Smart Connect経由.
guide.rate_limit: Not documented. Partner agreement specifies limits.

name: MakeLeaps
description: Invoice and quote management by Ricoh. Create, send, and track invoices. REST API for business partners and invoice documents. Popular with SMBs.

name: Stripe Japan
description: Payment processing platform. Full payment API with PayPay integration for Japan. Multiple third-party MCP servers available.

name: board (請求書管理)
description: Billing and contract management SaaS by Velc. API available for invoice operations, integrates with Google Apps Script.

name: SmartHR
description: No.1 HR management platform in Japan. Employee onboarding, document management, social insurance procedures, annual adjustment (年末調整). Comprehensive REST API with sandbox environment.

guide.auth_overview: OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code flow. Register application at developer.smarthr.jp to obtain client_id and client_secret. Exchange authorization code for access_token via POST /oauth/token. Access tokens expire in 1 hour; use refresh_token to renew.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Register at developer.smarthr.jp/apps. 2. Set redirect_uri. 3. Use sandbox environment for testing. 4. Request production access after verification.
agent_tips_sample: Employee names are in separate last_name/first_name fields. Japanese order: last_name first (田中 太郎).
Date fields use ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD), interpreted as JST.
Sandbox has pre-populated test employees — no need to create test data.
guide.rate_limit: 600 requests/10 minutes per access token. HTTP 429 with Retry-After header.

name: KING OF TIME
description: No.1 attendance management system in Japan. Clock in/out, shift management, overtime calculation, leave requests. REST API available. Japanese labor law compliant.

guide.auth_overview: API Token. Generate at KING OF TIME admin console > Settings > API. Include as Authorization: Bearer {token} header.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Admin login to KING OF TIME. 2. Settings > 外部サービス連携 > API. 3. Generate access token. 4. Use as Bearer token.

endpoints_sample.description: Get all employees' attendance for a date

agent_tips_sample: Date format: YYYY-MM-DD. All times are in JST (UTC+9).
Overtime is in minutes, not hours. 60 = 1 hour overtime.
Read-only API in most plans. Write operations (clock in/out) require premium API access.
guide.rate_limit: Not publicly documented. Recommended: max 10 requests/second.

name: Jobcan (ジョブカン)
description: 勤怠管理・経費精算・ワークフローの統合プラットフォーム。日本SMB市場でNo.1クラスのシェア。打刻・シフト管理・残業計算・有給管理。API for attendance and expense (beta). SAML SSO via Okta.

guide.auth_overview: API token authentication. Generate in ジョブカン管理画面 > API設定. Pass as Authorization: Token {token}.
guide.setup_hint: 1. ジョブカン管理画面にログイン. 2. 設定 > その他 > API設定. 3. APIトークンを発行. 4. Authorization: Token {token} ヘッダーで利用.
agent_tips_sample: ジョブカンは勤怠/ワークフロー/経費/採用/労務の5製品 — APIは勤怠がメイン.
打刻データは日本時間 (JST) で返される.
従業員IDは社員番号ではなくジョブカン内部ID — マッピングが必要.
guide.rate_limit: Not officially documented. Recommended: max 60 req/min.

name: Kaonavi (カオナビ)
description: No.1 talent management platform in Japan. Employee profiles, skill matrix, evaluation, succession planning. API v2 available.

guide.auth_overview: OAuth 2.0 client credentials. consumer_key + consumer_secret from 管理画面. Access token valid for 24 hours.
guide.setup_hint: 1. カオナビ管理画面 > 設定 > API設定. 2. consumer_key/secret を取得. 3. POST /token with grant_type=client_credentials + Basic auth (key:secret). 4. Bearer token利用.
agent_tips_sample: JP No.1タレントマネジメント — 3,000社以上導入.
Access tokenは24時間有効 — freee (24h) と同じパターン.
社員データは一括取得 → 個別詳細のパターン.
guide.rate_limit: 60 req/min. Token refresh: 1 req/min.

name: HRMOS勤怠 (ieyasu)
description: Attendance management by BizReach/Visional. Multiple clock-in methods (Slack, LINE, IC card). REST API for attendance data.

guide.auth_overview: API token authentication. Generate in HRMOS管理画面 > API設定. Pass as Bearer token.
guide.setup_hint: 1. HRMOS管理画面 > 設定 > API連携. 2. APIトークンを発行. 3. Authorization: Bearer {token}.
agent_tips_sample: HRMOS勤怠とHRMOS採用は別製品 — APIも別系統.
BizReachグループ（現Visional）提供.
従業員データは日本語フィールド名を含む場合あり.
guide.rate_limit: 60 req/min.

name: TeamSpirit
description: Attendance + expense management on Salesforce platform. Combines time tracking, expenses, and work scheduling. API via Salesforce.

guide.auth_overview: Salesforce OAuth 2.0. TeamSpiritはSalesforce Platform上に構築。Salesforce Connected Appで認証。
guide.setup_hint: 1. Salesforce設定 > アプリケーション > 接続アプリケーション > 新規. 2. client_id/secret取得. 3. OAuth フロー (username-password or auth code). 4. instance_urlに対してAPI呼び出し.

endpoints_sample.description: Get attendance records (勤怠レコード)

agent_tips_sample: Salesforce Platform上 — 全てのAPIはSalesforce REST/SOQL経由.
カスタムオブジェクト名は teamspirit__ プレフィックス付き.
SOQL必須 — TeamSpirit固有のオブジェクト・フィールド名を把握する必要あり.
guide.rate_limit: Salesforce limits: 100,000 API calls/24h (Enterprise). Concurrent: 25.

name: Chatwork MCP
description: Business messaging platform dominant in Japanese companies. Send messages, manage rooms, share files, create tasks within chats. Official MCP server with npm package.

guide.auth_overview: API token (simplest) or OAuth 2.0. API token: generate at chatwork.com/service/packages/chatwork/subpackages/api/token. Include as X-ChatWorkToken header. OAuth: register app at developer.chatwork.com.
guide.setup_hint: Easiest: Go to chatwork.com > Settings > API Token > generate. Use X-ChatWorkToken header. No app registration needed for personal token.

endpoints_sample.description: List chat rooms you belong to
Send a message to a room

agent_tips_sample: POST body uses application/x-www-form-urlencoded, NOT JSON. This is unusual — most agents default to JSON and get 400 errors.
Message body supports [info][title]Title[/title]Content[/info] tags for formatted messages. Use [To:account_id] for mentions.
Room IDs are numeric. Get them from GET /rooms first.
guide.rate_limit: 300 requests/5 minutes. HTTP 429 response. Check X-RateLimit-Remaining header.

name: LINE Messaging MCP
description: LINE official bot messaging. Push/broadcast messages, flex messages, user profiles. Official MCP server by LINE (preview status).

guide.auth_overview: Channel Access Token. Create a LINE Messaging API channel in LINE Developers Console. Use the long-lived or stateless channel access token in Authorization: Bearer header.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Go to developers.line.biz. 2. Create Provider > Create Messaging API Channel. 3. Issue channel access token (long-lived). 4. Set webhook URL for receiving messages.

endpoints_sample.description: Send push message to a user
Reply to a user message (via replyToken)

agent_tips_sample: User IDs start with 'U' and are 33 chars. They're channel-specific — same user has different IDs across channels.
Reply tokens expire in 1 minute. For async responses, use push message instead of reply.
Flex Messages (v2): rich interactive layouts. JSON structure is complex — use the Flex Message Simulator to build templates.
guide.rate_limit: Push: 100,000/min. Reply: unlimited. Broadcast: depends on plan (free plan: 200/month total messages).

name: LINE WORKS
description: Enterprise messaging by LINE/Works Mobile. Bot API, Calendar API, Drive API available. Dominant business messenger in Japan. No MCP server yet.

guide.auth_overview: OAuth 2.0 with JWT assertion. Service account issues JWT, exchanges for access token. Developer Console for client_id and service account.
guide.setup_hint: 1. LINE WORKS Developer Console > App登録. 2. Service Account発行 + Private Key ダウンロード. 3. JWTを生成 (iss=client_id, sub=service_account_id). 4. POST /token with grant_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:jwt-bearer.
agent_tips_sample: JWT auth is mandatory for API 2.0 — no simple API key option.
Private key must be RSA 2048-bit. Use jsonwebtoken library to sign JWTs.
Bot must be registered and added to channels before sending messages.
guide.rate_limit: 200 req/sec per app. Messaging: 5,000 messages/day for free plan.

name: Slack MCP
description: Business messaging platform. Native MCP server available (2026). Channels, DMs, threads, reactions, file sharing. Widely used in Japanese tech companies.

guide.auth_overview: OAuth 2.0 with Bot Token. Install Slack app to workspace, get xoxb- bot token. Include as Bearer token in Authorization header. Or use official Slack MCP server.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Create app at api.slack.com/apps. 2. Add Bot Token Scopes (chat:write, channels:read, etc.). 3. Install to workspace. 4. Copy xoxb- Bot User OAuth Token. Or use MCP: npx @anthropic/slack-mcp.

endpoints_sample.description: Send a message to a channel
List channels the bot can see
Get message history from a channel

agent_tips_sample: CRITICAL: Slack API always returns HTTP 200 even on errors. Always check response.ok field, not HTTP status code.
Channel IDs start with C (public), G (private group), D (DM). Use conversations.list to get IDs — channel names don't work in API calls.
Message timestamps ('ts') are unique message IDs. Use them for replies (thread_ts), reactions, and updates.
guide.rate_limit: Tier-based: Tier 1 (1/min), Tier 2 (20/min), Tier 3 (50/min), Tier 4 (100/min). chat.postMessage is Tier 3. HTTP 429 with Retry-After.

name: Microsoft Teams MCP
description: Enterprise communication by Microsoft. MCP via Teams AI Library (public preview). Chat, meetings, channels. Graph API integration.

guide.auth_overview: OAuth 2.0 via Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD). Supports authorization code flow, client credentials (app-only), and device code flow. Register app at Azure Portal > App registrations. Access token as Bearer. Official MCP via Teams AI Library (public preview).
guide.setup_hint: 1. Azure Portal > App registrations > New registration. 2. Add API permissions: Microsoft Graph > Delegated (or Application for daemons). 3. Admin consent required for most Teams scopes. 4. Generate client secret or certificate. 5. Or use: npx @microsoft/teams-mcp for managed setup.

endpoints_sample.description: List teams the user is a member of
Post a message to a channel

agent_tips_sample: Always honor Retry-After header on 429 — Microsoft Graph enforces it strictly.
For bot/daemon scenarios, use application permissions (client credentials flow) — no user login required.
Teams messaging requires RSC (Resource-Specific Consent) or admin consent for most write scopes.
guide.rate_limit: Per-app and per-tenant throttling. Teams messaging: ~30 requests/sec per app per tenant. Graph overall: ~10,000 requests per 10 min per app per tenant. HTTP 429 with Retry-After header.

name: Zoom
description: Video conferencing and team chat. Community MCP servers available. Meeting management, recordings, chat API.

name: Talknote
description: Japanese workplace communication tool with mood/engagement analytics. API documentation available at developer portal.

name: Backlog MCP
description: Project management by Nulab. Issue tracking, sprints, bug management, wiki, Git/PR management. Official MCP server. MIT license. Popular in Japanese dev teams.

guide.auth_overview: Two methods: 1) API Key — generate at Backlog settings, pass as ?apiKey= query param. 2) OAuth 2.0 — register at developer.nulab.com. API key is simpler for agents.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Log into Backlog > Personal Settings > API. 2. Generate API key. 3. Append ?apiKey={key} to every request URL. No header needed.

endpoints_sample.description: List issues with filters (project, status, assignee)

agent_tips_sample: POST/PATCH use application/x-www-form-urlencoded, not JSON. Common source of 400 errors.
Issue keys like 'PROJ-1' can be used instead of numeric IDs in most endpoints.
Array params use bracket notation: projectId[]=1&projectId[]=2.
guide.rate_limit: Varies by plan. Typically no strict rate limit for API key access, but heavy use may be throttled.

name: kintone MCP
description: Business application platform by Cybozu. Build custom apps, manage records, process workflows. Official MCP server. Available via Docker, npm, or Desktop Extension.

guide.auth_overview: Three auth methods: 1) API Token (per-app, simplest), 2) Password auth (username:password base64 in X-Cybozu-Authorization header), 3) OAuth 2.0 (register at developer.cybozu.io). API Token is recommended for agent use.
guide.setup_hint: Easiest: In kintone app settings > API Token > generate token with needed permissions. Include as X-Cybozu-API-Token header. For multiple apps, combine tokens with comma.

endpoints_sample.description: Get records from an app (supports query)
Add new records to an app

agent_tips_sample: Every field value is wrapped in {"value": "..."} — even numbers. Always access record.field_code.value.
Field codes (フィールドコード) are the API names — different from display labels. Get them from /app/form/fields.json first.
Base URL requires customer's subdomain: https://{subdomain}.cybozu.com. Ask the user for their subdomain.
guide.rate_limit: 10,000 requests/day for standard plan. No per-minute limit documented, but concurrent connections limited to 10.

name: Asana MCP
description: Task and project management. Official MCP server via developers.asana.com. Tasks, projects, sections, custom fields.

guide.auth_overview: OAuth 2.0 or Personal Access Token (PAT). PAT for quick setup — generate in Developer Console. OAuth for production apps.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Developer Console > My Apps > Create Token (PAT). 2. Use Authorization: Bearer {token}. 3. For OAuth: register app, get client_id/secret, auth code flow.
agent_tips_sample: All response data is wrapped in {data: ...} — always unwrap.
Use opt_fields query param to select specific fields and reduce payload.
GIDs (global IDs) are strings, not numbers — treat as strings.
guide.rate_limit: 1,500 req/min per PAT. Free tier: 150 req/min. Retry-After header on 429.

name: Jooto
description: Kanban-style project management. Boards, tasks, labels, progress tracking. Simple UI designed for Japanese teams.

name: Trello
description: Kanban board by Atlassian. Community MCP server with rate limiting and error handling. Boards, lists, cards, checklists.

name: Redmine
description: Open source issue tracking. Dockerized MCP server available via HTTP/SSE. Issues, projects, time entries. Used by many Japanese enterprises.

name: Garoon MCP
description: Enterprise groupware by Cybozu. Schedule management and messaging. Official MCP server. Can combine with kintone MCP for full Cybozu suite.

guide.auth_overview: Cybozu共通認証: API token or Basic Auth (パスワード認証) or OAuth. kintoneと同じ認証パターン.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Garoon管理画面 > APIトークン設定. 2. 必要な権限でトークン生成. 3. X-Cybozu-API-Token: {token} ヘッダー. 4. 複数トークンはカンマ結合.

endpoints_sample.description: Get schedule events (スケジュール)
Get workflow requests (ワークフロー)

agent_tips_sample: kintoneと認証方式が同じ — X-Cybozu-API-Tokenヘッダー.
日時はISO 8601 + タイムゾーン必須 (+09:00 for JST).
スケジュール + ワークフロー + 掲示板 + スペースの4機能がメイン.
guide.rate_limit: 10,000 req/day (standard). Concurrent: 10.

name: Notion MCP
description: All-in-one workspace for docs, project management, and knowledge base. Official MCP server for pages, databases, blocks, comments. Hosted server for secure workspace access. タスク管理・ナレッジベース・Wiki。

guide.auth_overview: Internal integration token (simplest) or OAuth 2.0. Create integration at notion.so/my-integrations. Token starts with 'ntn_' or 'secret_'. Set Notion-Version header.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Go to notion.so/my-integrations. 2. Create New Integration. 3. Copy Internal Integration Secret. 4. IMPORTANT: Share target pages/databases with the integration in Notion UI (click '...' > 'Connections' > add your integration).

endpoints_sample.description: Query a database with filters and sorts
Create a new page or database row

agent_tips_sample: CRITICAL: Integration must be explicitly shared with each page/database in Notion UI. Without this, API returns 404 even with valid IDs.
Notion-Version header is required. Use '2022-06-28' (latest stable).
Property values are deeply nested: properties.Name.title[0].text.content. Familiarize with the block/property type system.
guide.rate_limit: 3 requests/second average. Burst allowed. HTTP 429 with Retry-After.

name: rakumo
description: Google Workspace add-on for Japanese businesses. Calendar, workflow, board functions. HENNGE SSO integration.

name: Sansan MCP
description: Business card management and CRM. OCR scan, contact tracking, meeting history. Official MCP server (Nov 2025). Trialed at Sumitomo Corporation with M365 Copilot.

guide.auth_overview: OAuth 2.0 (since March 2026, replacing API key auth). Register at developer.sansan.com. Client credentials or Authorization Code flow.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Register at developer.sansan.com. 2. Create OAuth application. 3. Note: API key auth was removed in March 2026 — must use OAuth 2.0 now.
agent_tips_sample: BREAKING CHANGE (March 2026): Authentication switched from API key to OAuth 2.0. Old API keys no longer work.
Business card OCR accuracy is 98% — but always verify critical fields like email/phone for programmatic use.
Name search supports both kanji (田中) and reading (たなか). Use 'name' param for both.
guide.rate_limit: 1,000 requests/hour. HTTP 429 with Retry-After.

name: HubSpot Japan MCP
description: CRM, marketing automation, sales pipeline. Two official MCP servers. Deal tracking, contact management, email sequences.

guide.auth_overview: OAuth 2.0 or Private App access token. Private app token is simplest — generate in HubSpot Settings > Integrations > Private Apps. Use as Bearer token.
guide.setup_hint: Easiest: Settings > Integrations > Private Apps > Create. Select scopes needed. Copy access token. Use as: Authorization: Bearer {token}.
agent_tips_sample: Properties must be explicitly requested via 'properties' param — only default fields are returned otherwise.
Search API (POST /search) is more powerful than list with filters. Supports complex filter groups.
Deal pipeline stages are customizable per account. Get pipeline config via GET /crm/v3/pipelines/deals.
guide.rate_limit: Private apps: 100 requests/10 seconds. OAuth apps: 100/10s. HTTP 429 with Retry-After.

name: Salesforce Japan
description: Enterprise CRM with extensive REST/SOAP APIs. MCP restricted to Agentforce internal framework. Third-party MCP servers exist. Dominant in Japanese enterprise.

name: SALES GO (GoCoo!)
description: Japan's first MCP-enabled SFA (Oct 2025). 1/4 the cost of Salesforce. Sales activity management, pipeline, forecasting. GoZeeta AI agent planned.

guide.auth_overview: API key authentication. Generate in SalesGo管理画面. Pass as X-Api-Key header.
guide.setup_hint: 1. SalesGo管理画面 > 設定 > API連携. 2. APIキーを取得. 3. X-Api-Key: {key} ヘッダーで利用.
agent_tips_sample: JP初のMCP対応SFA — AIエージェント連携が差別化ポイント.
リード管理 + メール自動配信がメイン機能.
470万件の企業データベース内蔵 — ターゲティングに活用.
guide.rate_limit: Not documented.

name: Eight
description: Personal business card management app by Sansan. Eight Team has kintone API integration. Name card scanning and contact management.

guide.auth_overview: OAuth 2.0. Sansan提供の名刺管理個人向けサービス. Developer登録でアプリ作成.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Eight Developer Portalでアプリ登録. 2. OAuth認証フロー. 3. Authorization: Bearer {access_token}.
agent_tips_sample: Sansan提供の個人向け名刺管理 — Sansanは法人向け.
名刺データはOCR + 手入力で精度が高い.
個人の名刺データにPII含む — APPI準拠の取扱必須.
guide.rate_limit: Not documented. Recommended: 30 req/min.

name: Shopify Japan MCP
description: E-commerce platform with 4 official MCP servers (Dev, Storefront, Customer Account, Checkout). Every store exposes /api/mcp endpoint.

guide.auth_overview: OAuth 2.0 for custom apps, or Admin API access token for private apps. Create app in Shopify Partners or store admin. Access token in X-Shopify-Access-Token header.
guide.setup_hint: Easiest: Store admin > Settings > Apps > Develop apps > Create app > Configure API scopes > Install. Copy Admin API access token.
agent_tips_sample: GraphQL Admin API is preferred over REST for complex queries — single request instead of multiple REST calls.
Product prices are strings, not numbers ('1500.00'). Parse carefully.
API version is in the URL path. Use the latest stable version. Old versions are deprecated on a rolling basis.
guide.rate_limit: REST: 40 requests/second (2 per second for some plans). GraphQL: 1000 cost points/second. Check X-Shopify-Shop-Api-Call-Limit header.

name: BASE
description: Japanese e-commerce platform for small businesses. Product, order, shop information APIs. OAuth available. Easy store setup.

guide.auth_overview: OAuth 2.0. Register app at developers.thebase.in. Auth code flow for shop owner authorization.
guide.setup_hint: 1. developers.thebase.in でアプリ登録. 2. client_id/secret取得. 3. OAuth認証フロー: /1/oauth/authorize → code → /1/oauth/token. 4. Bearer token利用.
agent_tips_sample: POSTリクエストはJSON非対応 — form-urlencoded形式で送信.
Access tokenは30日で失効 — refresh_tokenで更新必須.
商品価格は税込み（内税）で管理されている.
guide.rate_limit: 3 req/sec per access token. Daily limit varies by plan.

name: STORES
description: E-commerce and POS platform. Online store, physical store POS, reservations, payments. Unified commerce for Japanese market. Developer API available.

name: Rakuten Ichiba (楽天市場)
description: Japan's largest online marketplace. Rakuten Web Service APIs (Ichiba, Books, Travel, Recipe). Community MCP for Rakuten Travel exists.

name: Amazon Japan (SP-API)
description: Amazon Selling Partner API for Japanese marketplace. Product listing, orders, fulfillment, reports. Third-party MCP servers exist.

name: CloudSign (クラウドサイン)
description: 日本No.1の電子契約サービス（弁護士ドットコム運営）。契約書の作成・送信・署名・管理。100以上のサービス連携。REST API available. No.1 electronic contract service in Japan.

guide.auth_overview: API Key + Client ID. Generate at CloudSign admin > Settings > API. Include X-Api-Key and X-Client-Id headers in every request.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Admin login to CloudSign. 2. Settings > API連携. 3. Generate API Key and Client ID. 4. Use both in request headers.

endpoints_sample.description: List all documents with status filter

agent_tips_sample: Requires BOTH X-Api-Key AND X-Client-Id headers. Missing either returns 401.
Document workflow: create → add participants → upload PDF → send → wait for signing.
Sandbox environment available — use it for testing. Documents in sandbox are not legally binding.
guide.rate_limit: 100 requests/minute. HTTP 429 on exceed.

name: GMO Sign (GMOサイン)
description: No.2 electronic contract service in Japan. API integration with CRM, internal systems, workflows. Government-grade security.

name: DocuSign Japan
description: Global e-signature platform. Community MCP servers available. eSignature API. Integrates with LegalOn Cloud.

name: LegalOn Cloud (LegalForce)
description: AI契約書レビュープラットフォーム。自動リスク検出、条項提案、契約書チェック。CloudSign・GMOサイン・DocuSign連携。AI contract review platform with automatic risk detection.

name: SendGrid
description: Email delivery and marketing platform by Twilio. Community MCP server available. v3 API for contacts, templates, campaigns, stats.

guide.auth_overview: Bearer token authentication with a SendGrid API key. Keys have three permission modes: Full Access, Restricted Access (granular per-endpoint), and Billing Access. Mail Send permission is required for the /mail/send endpoint.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Go to https://app.sendgrid.com/settings/api_keys and create a key with the `Mail Send` permission. 2. Verify your sender identity (domain or single sender) before sending — unverified senders return 403. 3. Store the key in SENDGRID_API_KEY env var.

endpoints_sample.description: Get email statistics for a date range

agent_tips_sample: A successful /mail/send returns 202 Accepted with an EMPTY body — there is no message ID in the response. To track delivery, enable Event Webhook or query /messages.
Domain authentication (SPF/DKIM/DMARC) dramatically improves deliverability — unauthenticated sends from shared IPs land in spam.
Use sandbox_mode during development — the request is validated but no email is sent.
guide.rate_limit: Mail Send: 1,000 requests/sec for Pro+; Free: 100/day. Marketing API: 100 requests/min. 429 with `X-RateLimit-Reset` header.

name: Treasure Data MCP
description: Customer data platform. Official open-source MCP server. Explore databases, analyze tables, write SQL via natural language. Joint PLAUD venture for Japan.

name: PLAUD Note
description: AI meeting recorder. MCP server for recordings, transcripts, AI summaries. Joint Treasure AI Voice venture with Treasure Data for Japan.

name: formrun
description: Form builder + customer management SaaS. Slack/Spreadsheet integration. Create forms, manage submissions, track customer inquiries.

name: Google Workspace
description: Productivity suite (Gmail, Drive, Calendar, Sheets, Docs). Multiple MCP servers for various services. Dominant in Japanese SMBs and startups.

guide.auth_overview: OAuth 2.0 with domain-wide delegation for admin, or per-user OAuth. Service account with domain-wide delegation for server apps. API key for public data only.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Google Cloud Console > Create project > Enable Workspace APIs. 2. Create OAuth client or service account. 3. For admin APIs: enable domain-wide delegation in Google Admin Console. 4. Set GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS for service accounts.
agent_tips_sample: Each Workspace API (Gmail, Calendar, Drive, Admin) has different scopes — request minimal scopes.
Domain-wide delegation requires Google Workspace admin to authorize the service account.
Use fields parameter to select specific response fields (same as opt_fields).
guide.rate_limit: Per-API: Admin SDK 2,400/min, Calendar 500/100s/user, Drive 12,000/min, Gmail 250 quota units/user/sec.

name: Misoca (ミソカ)
description: Invoice creation service by Yayoi/Intuit. Create, send, and manage invoices. REST API available. Popular with freelancers and small businesses in Japan.

guide.auth_overview: OAuth 2.0. Register app at 弥生IDの開発者ポータル. Misocaは弥生グループのため弥生IDで認証.
guide.setup_hint: 1. 弥生ID開発者ポータルでアプリ登録. 2. OAuth認証フローでaccess_token取得. 3. Authorization: Bearer {token}.
agent_tips_sample: 弥生グループのため弥生IDが必要 — Misoca単独のアカウントでは不可.
インボイス制度対応 — 適格請求書の発行に対応.
PDF出力はバイナリレスポンス — Content-Type: application/pdf.
guide.rate_limit: 30 req/min.

name: Wrike
description: Project management platform. Community MCP server with 8 production-ready API tools. Tasks, folders, projects, time tracking.

name: freee人事労務
description: HR and payroll management by freee. Employee data, attendance, payslips, year-end adjustment (年末調整). Covered by the same freee MCP server (28 API files for HR domain).

guide.auth_overview: Same OAuth 2.0 as freee Accounting (shared MCP server). Register app at freee Developer Portal. The freee MCP server (npx @freee/mcp-server) covers both accounting and HR domains — 28 API files for HR alone.
guide.setup_hint: Use the same freee app credentials as accounting. HR API shares the same OAuth token. Easiest: npx @freee/mcp-server handles both domains automatically.

endpoints_sample.description: Get employee detail (profile, dependents, bank account)

agent_tips_sample: Shares OAuth credentials with freee Accounting — same app, same token, same MCP server.
company_id required for all endpoints (same as accounting).
Employee names: last_name/first_name separate fields. Japanese order (姓→名).
guide.rate_limit: 3,600 requests/hour (shared with accounting API). HR endpoints: same rate pool.

name: Box Japan
description: Enterprise cloud content management and file sharing. No.1 enterprise file storage in Japan. Full REST API with OAuth 2.0. Supports metadata, workflows, and AI-powered content insights.

guide.auth_overview: OAuth 2.0 with JWT (server-to-server) or standard OAuth. JWT recommended for automation — no user interaction needed. Developer token for testing (60 min).
guide.setup_hint: 1. Create app at app.box.com/developers/console. 2. Choose OAuth 2.0 with JWT. 3. Generate key pair + download JSON config. 4. Admin must authorize the app in Admin Console. 5. Use Box SDK with JSON config.

endpoints_sample.description: List items in a folder (root: id=0)

agent_tips_sample: Root folder ID is always '0'. All folder operations start from here.
JWT auth requires Admin Console authorization — without it, API calls silently fail.
Use 'fields' query param to request specific fields and reduce payload.
guide.rate_limit: 1,000 API calls/min per user. Burst: up to 10 req/sec. Retry-After header on 429.

name: Google Drive
description: Cloud file storage and collaboration by Google. Part of Google Workspace but has independent Drive API v3. Upload, search, share, and manage files. OAuth 2.0.

name: Dropbox Business
description: Cloud storage and team collaboration. Dropbox Business API for file operations, team management, and sharing. Popular with design and creative teams in Japan.

guide.auth_overview: OAuth 2.0 with PKCE. Short-lived access tokens (4 hours) + refresh tokens. Scoped permissions via App Console.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Create app at dropbox.com/developers/apps. 2. Set permissions (scopes). 3. Generate access token for testing, or implement OAuth flow. 4. Use Authorization: Bearer {token}.
agent_tips_sample: All endpoints use POST (even reads) — Dropbox API is RPC-style, not REST.
File paths start with '/' and are case-insensitive. Root is '' (empty string).
Upload uses content-upload endpoint (content.dropboxapi.com) with Dropbox-API-Arg header.
guide.rate_limit: Not rate-limited per se, but 409 rate_limit errors for write-heavy operations. Automatic retry with Retry-After.

name: Zendesk
description: Customer support platform. Ticket management, help center, live chat. Comprehensive REST API. Widely used by Japanese SaaS companies for customer support.

guide.auth_overview: Three methods: 1) API Token — generate in Admin > Channels > API. Use as {email}/token:{api_token} with Basic Auth. 2) OAuth 2.0 for third-party apps. 3) Password auth (not recommended). API Token is simplest for agents.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Admin Center > Apps and integrations > APIs > Zendesk API. 2. Enable Token Access. 3. Add API Token. 4. Use Basic Auth: base64({email}/token:{api_token}).

endpoints_sample.description: List tickets with filters (status, assignee, etc.)

agent_tips_sample: Auth format is unusual: email/token:{api_token} (note the /token literal). Base64 encode the whole string for Basic Auth header.
Base URL requires customer's subdomain: https://{subdomain}.zendesk.com. Ask user for their Zendesk subdomain.
Search syntax: 'type:ticket status:open assignee:me' — supports complex queries with boolean operators.
guide.rate_limit: 400 requests/minute (Team), 700/min (Professional), 2500/min (Enterprise). HTTP 429 with Retry-After header.

name: Freshdesk
description: Cloud-based customer support software. Ticket management, automation, multi-channel support. REST API with API key auth. Growing adoption in Japan.

guide.auth_overview: API key authentication via Basic Auth. API key as username, 'X' as password. Get key from Profile Settings.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Login to Freshdesk > Profile icon > Profile Settings. 2. Copy API Key. 3. Use Basic Auth: base64({api_key}:X). 4. Or pass api_key as username in basic auth.
agent_tips_sample: Basic Auth password is literally 'X' (the letter) — not empty, not the key again.
Status values: 2=Open, 3=Pending, 4=Resolved, 5=Closed. Use numeric values.
Priority: 1=Low, 2=Medium, 3=High, 4=Urgent. Numeric only.
guide.rate_limit: Tier-based: Free (50/min), Growth (200/min), Pro (400/min), Enterprise (700/min).

name: KARTE
description: Japanese CX (Customer Experience) platform by PLAID. Real-time user analytics, web chat, pop-ups, push notifications. Datahub API for data integration. Used by major Japanese enterprises.

name: Intercom
description: Customer messaging platform. Live chat, chatbots, help center, product tours. Comprehensive REST API. Fin AI agent for automated support.

name: PAY.JP
description: Japanese online payment platform by PAY, Inc. Credit card processing, subscription billing, token-based payments. Simple REST API designed for Japanese developers.

name: GMOペイメントゲートウェイ
description: No.1 payment gateway in Japan. Credit card, convenience store payment, carrier billing, PayPay. Used by major EC sites. API available but documentation requires contract.

name: Square
description: POS, payments, online store, and invoicing platform. Comprehensive REST API and SDKs. Popular with small retail and food service in Japan. Square Terminal supports IC card payments.

guide.auth_overview: OAuth 2.0 or Personal Access Token. For agents, use Personal Access Token (generate in Square Developer Dashboard). Include as Bearer token in Authorization header.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Create app at developer.squareup.com. 2. For testing: use Sandbox Access Token (no real money). 3. For production: generate Personal Access Token in Developer Dashboard. 4. Use as: Authorization: Bearer {token}.

endpoints_sample.description: Search orders by date, status, etc.

agent_tips_sample: location_id is required for most endpoints — always call GET /locations first (same pattern as freee's company_id).
Sandbox available with separate base URL (squareupsandbox.com). Test payments without real charges.
Amounts are in the smallest currency unit. JPY is already integer, so ¥1000 = 1000 (no conversion needed, unlike USD).
guide.rate_limit: Varies by endpoint. Most: 30 requests/second. Batch endpoints: 10/second. HTTP 429 with Retry-After.

name: Twilio
description: Cloud communications platform. SMS, voice, video, email APIs. Japanese phone numbers available (+81). Programmable messaging with Japanese carrier support.

guide.auth_overview: HTTP Basic Auth with Account SID and Auth Token. Find both at twilio.com/console. Account SID starts with 'AC'. Auth Token is the secret.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Sign up at twilio.com. 2. Get Account SID and Auth Token from Console Dashboard. 3. Buy a Japanese phone number (+81). 4. Use Basic Auth: base64(AccountSID:AuthToken).
agent_tips_sample: POST body uses application/x-www-form-urlencoded, NOT JSON. Common mistake for agents.
Japanese phone numbers: +81 prefix, drop leading 0. Mobile: +8190/80/70XXXXXXXX.
SMS to Japanese mobile: 670 chars max. Single segment is 70 chars (Shift_JIS). Stay under 70 for lowest cost.
guide.rate_limit: SMS: 1 message/second per number (can increase). API: no strict rate limit but concurrent connections limited.

name: Amazon SES
description: Email sending service by AWS. Transactional and marketing emails. High deliverability. REST API or SMTP. Used for automated notifications in Japanese business workflows.

name: スマレジ
description: Cloud POS system for retail and food service in Japan. Inventory management, sales analytics, staff management. Platform API for full integration. 125,000+ registered stores.

name: RESERVA
description: Japanese online reservation system. Supports 40+ industries (medical, beauty, fitness, restaurant, etc.). REST API for booking management. 300,000+ registered businesses.

name: Airリザーブ
description: Reservation management by Recruit. Free plan available. Integrates with Hot Pepper Beauty, jalan. Limited API but connects via Airレジ ecosystem.

name: ヤマト運輸 B2クラウド
description: Japan's No.1 delivery service. B2 Cloud API for shipping label creation, tracking, and delivery status. Essential for Japanese e-commerce logistics.

name: 佐川急便 飛伝
description: Major Japanese delivery service. Hiden (飛伝) system for shipping management. API for label creation, tracking. Used by mid-large EC operations.

name: Yoom
description: Japanese iPaaS (Integration Platform as a Service). No-code workflow automation connecting 200+ Japanese and global SaaS. API available for programmatic workflow creation.

name: Zapier
description: Global iPaaS connecting 7,000+ apps. REST API for managing Zaps programmatically. Natural Language Actions API for AI agents. Widely used with Japanese SaaS.

guide.auth_overview: API key or OAuth. NLA API uses API key from zapier.com/l/natural-language-actions. Pass as Bearer token.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Go to zapier.com/l/natural-language-actions. 2. Enable actions (Gmail, Slack, etc.) for AI access. 3. Get API key. 4. Use Authorization: Bearer {key} or x-api-key header.

endpoints_sample.description: List exposed actions configured for AI
Execute an action with natural language

agent_tips_sample: NLA API is designed for AI agents — pass natural language instructions.
Each action must be manually 'exposed' by the user in Zapier dashboard.
Preview before execute: POST /exposed/{id}/execute/ with preview_only:true.
guide.rate_limit: Not officially documented. Dependent on connected service limits.

name: Make (Integromat)
description: Visual iPaaS for workflow automation. Supports complex branching, error handling, and data transformation. REST API. Strong adoption in Japanese tech companies.

name: Google Analytics (GA4)
description: Web and app analytics by Google. GA4 Data API and Admin API. Essential for marketing teams. Real-time reporting, audience insights, conversion tracking.

name: Sentry
description: Application error monitoring and performance tracking. REST API for issue management, release tracking, and alert configuration. Used by Japanese dev teams.

name: Datadog
description: Cloud monitoring and security platform. Metrics, logs, traces, and alerts. APM, infrastructure monitoring, log management. Comprehensive REST API. Japanese documentation available. Popular with SRE teams.

name: Tableau
description: BI and data visualization platform by Salesforce. Interactive dashboards, data prep, REST API and Metadata API. Japanese UI and documentation. Dominant in enterprise BI market.

name: Looker (Google Cloud)
description: Enterprise BI platform by Google Cloud. LookML modeling, embedded analytics, comprehensive REST API. Strong adoption in data-driven Japanese companies.

name: Metabase
description: Open-source BI tool. Self-hosted or cloud. Simple SQL queries, dashboards, and alerts. REST API for programmatic access. Popular with startups and dev teams in Japan.

name: HENNGE One
description: Cloud security and identity platform for Japanese enterprises. SSO, email security (HENNGE Email DLP), access control. Admin API for user and policy management. Dominant in JP cloud security.

guide.auth_overview: Admin API with API token. Generate in HENNGE One管理コンソール. Limited public API availability.
guide.setup_hint: 1. HENNGE One管理コンソール > API設定. 2. APIトークン発行. 3. 注意: APIは管理者向けで限定的. SSO/セキュリティ設定の自動化が主な用途.
agent_tips_sample: JP No.1 クラウドセキュリティ (SaaS認証基盤) — IdP + メールセキュリティ.
APIは限定的 — ユーザー管理とポリシー設定がメイン.
SSO (SAML/OIDC) 連携の設定自動化に有用.
guide.rate_limit: Not documented.

name: Auth0
description: Identity platform by Okta. Authentication, authorization, SSO, MFA. Management API and Authentication API. Used by many Japanese SaaS for customer identity.

name: 1Password Business
description: Enterprise password and secrets management. Connect Server API for automation. SCIM provisioning. Events API for audit logs. Growing adoption in Japanese enterprise IT.

name: SATORI
description: Japanese marketing automation platform. Anonymous lead tracking, form builder, email campaigns, scoring. API for lead and campaign management. Strong in Japanese B2B marketing.

guide.auth_overview: API key authentication. Generate in SATORI管理画面. Pass as X-Api-Key header.
guide.setup_hint: 1. SATORI管理画面 > 設定 > API設定. 2. APIキーを生成. 3. X-Api-Key: {api_key} ヘッダー.
agent_tips_sample: JP国産MA (Marketing Automation) — 匿名リードのトラッキングが強み.
リードスコアリング機能 — score値でホットリード判定.
Webトラッキングコード設置が前提 — Cookie連携で行動分析.
guide.rate_limit: Not documented.

name: b→dash
description: All-in-one customer data platform (CDP). MA, BI, web analytics in one. Data palette (no-code data integration). Growing rapidly in Japanese mid-market. 顧客データ基盤。

guide.auth_overview: API key authentication. Generate in b→dash管理画面. Primarily data integration via ETL, not REST API.
guide.setup_hint: 1. b→dash管理画面 > データ連携設定. 2. API認証キー取得. 3. 注意: REST APIは限定的 — ノーコードETLがメインの連携方法.
agent_tips_sample: CDP (Customer Data Platform) + MA一体型 — データ統合がメイン.
REST APIは限定的 — ノーコードGUIでのETL連携がメインの使い方.
SQLベースのセグメンテーション — 分析にはSQL知識必要.
guide.rate_limit: Not documented.

name: Marketo (Adobe)
description: Enterprise marketing automation by Adobe. Lead management, email campaigns, analytics, ABM. Comprehensive REST API. Dominant in Japanese enterprise MA market.

guide.auth_overview: OAuth 2.0 client credentials. Get client_id + client_secret from Admin > Integration > LaunchPoint. Access token valid for 1 hour.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Admin > Integration > LaunchPoint > New Service. 2. Note client_id and client_secret. 3. GET /identity/oauth/token?grant_type=client_credentials&client_id={id}&client_secret={secret}. 4. Use access_token as Bearer.

endpoints_sample.description: Get leads by filter (email, id, etc.)

agent_tips_sample: Munchkin ID is in your Marketo URL — e.g., 123-ABC-456.mktorest.com.
Access tokens expire in 1 hour — cache and refresh proactively.
Leads.json action can be 'createOrUpdate' (default), 'createOnly', 'updateOnly'.
guide.rate_limit: 50,000 API calls/day (standard). 10 concurrent API calls. 100 calls per 20 seconds.

name: バクラク請求書 (LayerX)
description: AI-powered invoice processing by LayerX. OCR auto-reading, approval workflow, accounting integration. API for invoice data extraction and status management. Rapidly growing in Japan.

guide.auth_overview: API key authentication. Limited API availability — primarily webhook and CSV-based integration.
guide.setup_hint: 1. バクラク管理画面 > 設定 > API連携. 2. APIキーを発行. 3. 注意: REST APIは限定的、主にCSV連携とWebhook.
agent_tips_sample: AI-OCRが強み — 請求書の自動読取がメイン機能.
REST APIは限定的 — Webhook連携 + CSVエクスポートが主な連携方法.
freeeやMFとの会計連携が前提の設計.
guide.rate_limit: Not documented.

name: invox受取請求書
description: AI-OCR invoice receiving service. Automatic data extraction from PDF/paper invoices. API for invoice data retrieval. Integrates with major accounting software. Supports Japanese Invoice System (インボイス制度).

name: Talentio
description: Cloud-based ATS (Applicant Tracking System) for Japanese companies. Recruitment pipeline, candidate management, interview scheduling. API for candidate and job data.

guide.auth_overview: API key authentication. Generate in Talentio管理画面. Pass as Bearer token.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Talentio管理画面 > 設定 > API. 2. APIキーを生成. 3. Authorization: Bearer {api_key}.
agent_tips_sample: ATS (採用管理) 特化 — 応募者トラッキングがメイン.
選考ステージは企業カスタム — stages APIで事前取得必要.
応募者データにPII含む — 取扱注意.
guide.rate_limit: Not officially documented. Recommended: 30 req/min.

name: オフィスステーション
description: Cloud-based social insurance and labor management platform. e-Gov API integration for electronic filing. Employee data management, year-end adjustment. API available for enterprise plans.

guide.auth_overview: API token authentication. 管理画面から発行. Limited API — primarily CSV export and e-Gov連携.
guide.setup_hint: 1. オフィスステーション管理画面 > API設定. 2. トークン発行. 3. Authorization: Bearer {token}. 注意: API範囲は限定的.
agent_tips_sample: 社会保険・労務手続きに特化 — e-Gov電子申請がメイン機能.
APIは限定的 — CSV連携やe-Gov直接連携が主な利用パターン.
マイナンバー管理機能あり — PIIの取扱に厳重注意.
guide.rate_limit: Not documented.

name: EC-CUBE
description: Open-source EC platform dominant in Japan. Self-hosted or cloud. Plugin ecosystem. REST API for products, orders, customers. Powers many Japanese online shops.

name: カラーミーショップ
description: Popular hosted EC platform by GMO Pepabo. Easy store setup for Japanese SMBs. REST API for products, orders, and customers. Over 40,000 active shops.

name: TableCheck
description: Restaurant reservation and guest management platform. Used by premium restaurants in Japan. REST API for reservations, guest profiles, and table management.

name: SELECTTYPE
description: Multi-purpose reservation system. Event, facility, and service booking. Embeddable calendar widget. API for reservation management. Popular with salons, clinics, and studios in Japan.

name: 日本郵便 (ゆうパックプリントR)
description: Japan Post shipping label service. ゆうパックプリントR for batch label creation. CSV import/export API. Essential for EC businesses in Japan alongside ヤマト and 佐川.

name: ロジレス (LOGILESS)
description: Cloud OMS+WMS for EC fulfillment. Order management, inventory, warehouse operations in one. API for orders, inventory, and shipping. Integrates with major EC platforms and carriers in Japan.

name: チャネルトーク (Channel Talk)
description: All-in-one customer communication platform. Live chat, chatbot, CRM, marketing. REST API and webhook. Rapidly growing in Japanese D2C and SaaS customer support.

guide.auth_overview: API key + secret pair. Pass as X-Access-Key and X-Access-Secret headers. Generate in Channel Talk Settings > Developer.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Channel Talk 管理画面 > 設定 > デベロッパー. 2. API Key (Access Key) + Secret を取得. 3. Set X-Access-Key and X-Access-Secret headers.
agent_tips_sample: Dual header auth: both X-Access-Key AND X-Access-Secret required.
User chats vs team chats — user-chats are customer conversations, team-chats are internal.
Webhook for real-time: Settings > Developer > Webhook URL.
guide.rate_limit: Not officially documented. Observed ~100 req/sec.

name: LINE Pay
description: Mobile payment by LINE. QR code payment, online payment, transit payment. Request API and Inquiry API. Dominant in Japanese mobile payments alongside PayPay.

name: Paidy
description: Buy now, pay later (BNPL) platform for Japan by PayPal. Email+phone authentication, no credit card needed. Merchant API for payment creation and management. Growing in Japanese EC.

name: Confluence
description: Team wiki and knowledge base by Atlassian. Pages, spaces, templates, macros. Comprehensive REST API and Connect framework. Standard in Japanese enterprise documentation.

name: ClickUp
description: All-in-one project management and productivity platform. Tasks, docs, goals, whiteboards, time tracking. Comprehensive REST API. Growing adoption in Japanese startups and dev teams.

guide.auth_overview: OAuth 2.0 or personal API token. Personal token for quick setup — generate in Settings > Apps. OAuth for production integrations.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Settings > Apps > Generate API Token (personal). 2. Use Authorization: {token} (no Bearer prefix!). 3. For OAuth: register app, get client_id/secret, redirect flow.

endpoints_sample.description: Create a task in a list

agent_tips_sample: Auth header is Authorization: {token} — NO 'Bearer' prefix for personal tokens.
Hierarchy: Workspace > Space > Folder > List > Task. Navigate top-down.
Custom fields are in custom_fields array — use field ID, not name, for updates.
guide.rate_limit: 100 req/min per token. 429 with Retry-After header.

name: Todoist
description: Task management app with natural language input. Projects, labels, filters, reminders. REST API and Sync API. MCP server available. Popular personal/team task tool in Japan.

name: freeeサイン
description: Electronic contract service by freee (formerly Ninja Sign). Contract creation, signing, management. API for contract operations. Integrates with freee accounting ecosystem.

guide.auth_overview: OAuth 2.0 via freee共通認証基盤. freee会計と同じOAuthフロー.
guide.setup_hint: 1. freee API設定 > アプリ登録 (sign権限を含む). 2. OAuth認証フロー. 3. freee会計と同一のaccess_tokenでsign APIも利用可能（スコープ設定による）.
agent_tips_sample: freee会計と認証基盤が共通 — 1つのOAuthアプリでsignも利用可能.
CloudSignの競合 — freeeエコシステム内での電子署名.
契約ステータス: draft → sent → signed → completed.
guide.rate_limit: 300 req/5min (freee共通).

name: Zoho CRM
description: Cloud CRM with sales automation, analytics, and multi-channel support. Japanese UI and support. Comprehensive REST API. Cost-effective alternative to Salesforce for Japanese SMBs.

name: GitHub
description: World's largest code hosting platform. Repository management, issues, PRs, Actions, and more. Official MCP server available.

guide.auth_overview: OAuth 2.0 (user-to-server via GitHub App or OAuth App) and Personal Access Tokens (classic or fine-grained). Bearer token in Authorization header. Official MCP server (npx @github/mcp-server) handles auth via GitHub App installation or PAT.
guide.setup_hint: 1. For quickest start: Settings > Developer settings > Personal access tokens > Fine-grained tokens. 2. For production/org-wide: create GitHub App at Settings > Developer settings > GitHub Apps. 3. Or just use: npx @github/mcp-server (handles OAuth/installation).

endpoints_sample.description: List issues (supports state, labels, assignee filters)

agent_tips_sample: Always include X-GitHub-Api-Version: 2022-11-28 header for stable behavior.
Use fine-grained PATs over classic PATs — scoped to specific repos with least-privilege.
Rate limit is shared across all REST + GraphQL calls under the same token. Check X-RateLimit-Remaining header.
guide.rate_limit: 5,000 req/hour (authenticated PAT). GitHub App: 15,000/hour per installation. Search API: 30 req/min. Secondary rate limits on abuse detection.

name: GitLab
description: DevSecOps platform with built-in CI/CD, container registry, and security scanning. Official MCP server available for Duo integration.

guide.auth_overview: OAuth 2.0 (Authorization Code) or Personal/Project/Group Access Tokens. Bearer token in Authorization header, or PRIVATE-TOKEN header for PATs. Self-managed GitLab instances use the same schema at their own domain. Official MCP server for Duo integration: npx @gitlab/mcp-server.
guide.setup_hint: 1. User Settings > Access Tokens > Personal Access Token with 'api' scope (simplest). 2. Or: Preferences > Applications for OAuth apps. 3. For CI/CD: use Project Access Tokens. 4. Self-managed: replace gitlab.com with your instance domain. 5. Or use: npx @gitlab/mcp-server (Duo MCP).

endpoints_sample.description: List projects accessible to the authenticated user
List issues in a project (id is URL-encoded path or numeric ID)

agent_tips_sample: Project IDs can be numeric (fast) OR URL-encoded namespace/path (e.g., 'gitlab-org%2Fgitlab'). Prefer numeric for performance.
Issue 'iid' is per-project; 'id' is globally unique. Use iid when referencing from URLs, id for API calls.
For self-managed GitLab, swap gitlab.com with your instance — all endpoints and schemas are identical.
guide.rate_limit: GitLab.com: 2,000 req/min per authenticated user (unauthenticated: 500/min per IP). Project-level rate limits configurable on self-managed. HTTP 429 with RateLimit-Reset header.

name: Bitbucket
description: Git repository hosting by Atlassian. Tight integration with Jira, Confluence, and Bitbucket Pipelines CI/CD. REST API v2 for repos, PRs, and pipelines.

name: Vercel
description: Frontend cloud platform for deploying web applications. Optimized for Next.js. Serverless functions, edge network, and preview deployments.

guide.auth_overview: Bearer token authentication. Use a Personal Access Token (Settings > Tokens) for individual use, or OAuth 2.0 for multi-tenant integrations. Team endpoints require the `teamId` query parameter when operating on a team scope.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Go to https://vercel.com/account/tokens and create a token. 2. For team operations, pass `teamId=team_xxx` as a query parameter. 3. The official Vercel MCP adapter handles this automatically.

endpoints_sample.description: Create a new deployment from a Git source or files

agent_tips_sample: Team-scoped tokens still need `?teamId=team_xxx` appended to every call. Omitting it returns 403 on team resources.
Deployments are immutable once created — to "update" you must create a new deployment. Plan your agent flows accordingly.
Use `target=production` when creating deployments that should become the live URL; otherwise they become preview URLs.
guide.rate_limit: Personal tokens: varies by endpoint, typically 100 req/min per token. Team plans get higher limits. Deployments endpoint is stricter (~30/min). Exceeding returns 429 with Retry-After header.

name: Netlify
description: Web development platform for deploying static sites and serverless functions. Git-based workflow, forms, identity, and edge functions.

guide.auth_overview: Personal Access Token (recommended for agents) or OAuth 2.0 for multi-user apps. Bearer token in Authorization header. Tokens are scoped to the user's team memberships.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Go to https://app.netlify.com/user/applications/personal to create a personal access token. 2. For production integrations: register an OAuth application. 3. Official Netlify MCP (`npx -y @netlify/mcp`) handles token exchange.

endpoints_sample.description: List all sites for the authenticated user/team

agent_tips_sample: Deploys from zip uploads need Content-Type: application/zip and the raw binary body — not JSON.
Form submissions are accessible only if the site has form detection enabled in site settings.
Use `prod=true` query param when triggering a deploy to promote it to the production URL.
guide.rate_limit: 500 requests/minute per access token for most endpoints. Deploys endpoint stricter. 429 with Retry-After header when exceeded.

name: Railway
description: Cloud platform for deploying apps, databases, and cron jobs. Simple Git-based deploys with automatic environment management. GraphQL API.

name: Render
description: Cloud platform for hosting web services, static sites, cron jobs, and databases. Auto-deploy from Git with free SSL and global CDN.

guide.auth_overview: API key authentication. Generate at dashboard.render.com Account Settings. Pass as Bearer token in Authorization header.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Go to dashboard.render.com → Account Settings → API Keys. 2. Create new key. 3. Use Authorization: Bearer {key}.
agent_tips_sample: Service types: web_service, private_service, background_worker, static_site, cron_job.
Deploy hooks are simpler for CI/CD — no auth needed, just POST to webhook URL.
Check service.suspended field — suspended services won't deploy.
guide.rate_limit: Not officially documented. Observed ~100 req/min.

name: Fly.io
description: Platform for running full-stack apps and containers globally close to users. Firecracker VMs, edge deployment, container orchestration, built-in Postgres. Machines API for programmatic control.

guide.auth_overview: API token authentication. Generate via flyctl CLI (fly tokens create) or dashboard. Pass as Bearer token.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Install flyctl: curl -L https://fly.io/install.sh | sh. 2. fly auth login. 3. fly tokens create for API token. 4. Use Authorization: Bearer {token}.
agent_tips_sample: Machines API is the primary API — Fly Apps v1 API is deprecated.
Use region 'nrt' (Narita/Tokyo) for JP workloads.
Machines are per-request VMs — they can start/stop in <300ms.
guide.rate_limit: Not officially documented. Generous limits for Machine operations.

name: OpenAI API
description: AI models API including GPT-4, DALL-E, Whisper, and embeddings. Chat completions, assistants, and function calling. The most widely used LLM API.

guide.auth_overview: API key authentication. Generate at platform.openai.com/api-keys. Pass as Bearer token. Organization header optional for multi-org accounts.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Sign up at platform.openai.com. 2. Create API key (project-scoped recommended). 3. Set Authorization: Bearer {key}. 4. Optional: OpenAI-Organization header for org routing.
agent_tips_sample: Use gpt-4o for best quality/speed balance, gpt-4o-mini for cost efficiency.
Always set stream:true for chat UIs — reduces perceived latency.
Project-scoped API keys are more secure than user-level keys.
guide.rate_limit: Tier-based: Tier 1 (500 RPM), Tier 2 (5,000 RPM), up to Tier 5. Per-model TPM limits. Check headers: x-ratelimit-remaining-*.

name: Anthropic API
description: Claude AI models API. Chat completions, tool use, vision, and extended thinking. Known for safety and long-context capabilities.

name: Replicate
description: Platform to run open-source ML models in the cloud. Thousands of models for image, video, audio, and text generation. Simple API with pay-per-use pricing.

name: Hugging Face
description: Open-source AI community and platform. Model hub, datasets, Spaces, and Inference API. Hosts hundreds of thousands of ML models and datasets.

guide.auth_overview: Bearer token authentication with a Hugging Face User Access Token (hf_...). Tokens have scopes (read, write, inference) configurable at creation. The Inference API accepts the same token. For dedicated endpoints, use the Inference Endpoints API with the same token.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Go to https://huggingface.co/settings/tokens and create a token with the scopes you need. 2. For inference, choose "Read" + "Make calls to the serverless Inference API". 3. Set HF_TOKEN env var for the huggingface_hub Python library to pick it up.

endpoints_sample.description: Search/list models with filter by task, language, etc.
Run inference on a Serverless Inference model

agent_tips_sample: First call to a Serverless Inference model may return 503 with `estimated_time` — the model is cold-starting. Retry after the specified delay.
Set `x-wait-for-model: true` header to automatically block until the model is ready instead of getting 503.
Model IDs use the format `{owner}/{name}` (e.g., `meta-llama/Llama-3-8b`). Private model access requires an HF token with read scope to that repo.
guide.rate_limit: Serverless Inference: free tier has strict per-minute/hour limits (varies by model). Pro tier: higher limits. Dedicated endpoints bypass this limit entirely. 503 with `X-Compute-Time` header indicates cold start — not rate limiting.

name: Pinecone
description: Managed vector database for AI applications. High-performance similarity search, filtering, and real-time updates. Essential for RAG and semantic search.

guide.auth_overview: API key authentication via the Api-Key header (NOT Authorization). Each Pinecone project has its own API keys. The control plane (api.pinecone.io) manages indexes; the data plane has per-index URLs like https://{index-name}-{project-id}.svc.{environment}.pinecone.io/.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Go to https://app.pinecone.io and create a project. 2. Copy the API key from the API Keys tab. 3. For data ops you also need the index host URL — get it from the console or from GET /indexes/{name}.

endpoints_sample.description: Create a new serverless or pod-based index
Get index metadata including data-plane host

agent_tips_sample: Data plane and control plane use DIFFERENT base URLs. Always call GET /indexes/{name} first to resolve the data-plane host, then cache it.
The auth header is `Api-Key: {key}` — it is NOT Authorization/Bearer. This trips up most generic HTTP clients.
Vector dimension must match the index dimension exactly — a mismatch returns 400 with "Vector dimension X does not match index dimension Y".
guide.rate_limit: Serverless: scales automatically, billed per operation (no hard per-minute cap). Pod-based: limited by pod size and replica count. Control plane: ~100 requests/min for admin endpoints.

name: Weaviate
description: Open-source vector database with built-in vectorization modules. Supports hybrid search, multi-tenancy, and generative search. Self-hosted or cloud.

name: LangChain / LangSmith
description: Framework for building LLM-powered applications. LangSmith provides observability, testing, and evaluation for LLM apps. REST API for traces and datasets.

name: Supabase
description: Open-source Firebase alternative. Postgres database, auth, storage, edge functions, and realtime subscriptions. MCP server available for database management.

name: Firebase
description: Google's app development platform. Firestore, Authentication, Cloud Functions, Hosting, and Analytics. Comprehensive SDKs and REST APIs for web and mobile.

guide.auth_overview: Google OAuth 2.0 with service account or user credentials. Service accounts use JWT-based access tokens (ADC). Admin SDK is preferred for server-side agent use. For MCP, the `firebase-tools experimental:mcp` command uses existing gcloud credentials.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Create a service account in Google Cloud Console > IAM & Admin > Service Accounts. 2. Download the JSON key and set GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS. 3. Run `firebase login` for interactive use. 4. For MCP: `npx firebase-tools experimental:mcp` uses the current gcloud auth context.
agent_tips_sample: The Firebase REST Management API only manages project metadata — for actual data ops use Firestore REST (/v1/projects/{p}/databases/{db}/documents).
Always prefer the Admin SDK over REST on the server side — it handles token refresh and connection pooling.
Security rules are deployed separately from data — an agent that writes "correctly" may still hit PERMISSION_DENIED if rules are strict.
guide.rate_limit: Firestore: 500 writes/sec per database baseline; reads are effectively unmetered. Auth: 3,000 requests/min. Management API: 600 req/min per project. 429 or 403 quotaExceeded errors on overload.

name: MongoDB Atlas
description: Fully managed cloud MongoDB service. Document database with Atlas Search, vector search, and serverless instances. Atlas Admin and Data API.

guide.auth_overview: Digest authentication with a Programmatic API Key (public+private key pair) is the Atlas Admin API standard. For data access, use the MongoDB driver with a connection string (SRV URI). The MongoDB MCP server (`mongodb-mcp-server`) accepts a connection string directly.
guide.setup_hint: 1. In Atlas, go to Organization > Access Manager > API Keys. 2. Create a key pair with the required Atlas roles. 3. Whitelist your agent's IP/CIDR range. 4. For data queries, create a database user under Project > Database Access and use the SRV connection string.

endpoints_sample.description: List all clusters in a project

agent_tips_sample: Atlas Admin API uses HTTP Digest auth — not Bearer. This is an uncommon choice and breaks many HTTP clients; use a library that supports it.
Always set the `Accept: application/vnd.atlas.YYYY-MM-DD+json` header to pin an API version.
Data-plane operations (CRUD against collections) do NOT go through this API — use a MongoDB driver with the SRV connection string instead.
guide.rate_limit: Atlas Admin API: 100 requests/minute per API key. Returns 429 with Retry-After header. Data plane (via MongoDB driver) is limited by cluster size, not by API key.

name: PlanetScale
description: Serverless MySQL-compatible database platform. Branching workflows, non-blocking schema changes, and query insights. REST and gRPC APIs.

guide.auth_overview: Service Token authentication (tokens from Organization > Service Tokens), sent in the Authorization header as `Bearer {token_id}:{token_secret}`. Data plane uses MySQL protocol with username/password credentials generated per database.
guide.setup_hint: 1. In the PlanetScale console, go to Organization > Service Tokens > New. 2. Grant the token access to specific databases. 3. For data access, create a password under Database > Connect and use the MySQL SRV-like connection string. 4. Enable safe migrations mode before applying schema changes.
agent_tips_sample: Auth header format is unusual: `Authorization: {token_id}:{token_secret}` — NO "Bearer" prefix, just the two parts separated by a colon.
PlanetScale enforces schema changes via deploy requests (like PRs for databases) — direct ALTER TABLE on main is blocked.
Branches are free to create and destroy — use them for migration testing and PR previews.
guide.rate_limit: Management API: ~100 requests/min per service token. 429 with Retry-After header. Data plane is bound by compute plan (Scaler Pro and above have higher limits).

name: Neon
description: Serverless Postgres with branching, autoscaling, and bottomless storage. Instant database provisioning and scale-to-zero. REST API for management.

guide.base_url: https://console.neon.tech/api/v2/ (control plane) + postgres:// connection strings (data plane)
guide.auth_overview: Bearer token authentication for the control plane (Neon API Key from console). Data plane uses PostgreSQL wire protocol with connection strings — authentication is by database role/password. Neon supports pooled and direct connection strings.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Go to https://console.neon.tech and create a project. 2. Generate an API Key at Account Settings > API Keys. 3. For data access, copy the connection string from Project > Connection Details — note the difference between "pooled" (via pgBouncer) and "direct" URLs. 4. The Neon MCP server wraps both control and data planes.

endpoints_sample.description: Create a Neon project (creates a primary branch and compute)
List branches (Neon supports Git-like DB branching)
Create a new branch from an existing branch

agent_tips_sample: Neon's killer feature is DB branching — create a branch of production for testing migrations, then merge back. Branches are near-instant and copy-on-write.
ALWAYS use the POOLED connection string from serverless/edge functions — direct connections exhaust the connection limit fast.
Neon auto-suspends compute after inactivity (Free tier: 5 min). The first query after suspend takes 1-3 seconds to wake up.
guide.rate_limit: Control plane: 700 requests/minute per API key. Data plane: bound by compute size (Free tier: 0.25 CU = ~100 connections via pooled URL).

name: Upstash
description: Serverless Redis, Kafka, and QStash for developers. Pay-per-request pricing with global replication. REST API compatible with Redis commands.

guide.auth_overview: Basic authentication for the management API (email:api_key from account settings). Data plane uses per-database REST tokens: each database has a UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_URL and UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_TOKEN pair; the token goes in the Authorization: Bearer header.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Create an account at https://console.upstash.com. 2. Create a database (Redis or Kafka). 3. Copy UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_URL and UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_TOKEN from the database details page. 4. For admin ops (listing DBs), create an API key at Account > Management API.

endpoints_sample.description: [management] List all databases in the account

agent_tips_sample: Upstash's REST-over-HTTPS is designed for serverless/edge — no connection pooling needed, unlike regular Redis drivers.
Use /pipeline to batch multiple commands — one HTTP call instead of N, dramatically reduces latency on edge functions.
Commands with arguments are path-encoded: /set/{key}/{value}/EX/{ttl} — URL-encode values that contain special chars.
guide.rate_limit: Free tier: 10,000 commands/day per database, 1,000 requests/sec. Pay-as-you-go scales to millions. Per-second limits are enforced at the REST edge.

name: Discord
description: Communication platform for communities and teams. Bots, webhooks, slash commands, and rich interactions. Widely used by developer and gaming communities.

name: Telegram Bot API
description: Bot platform for Telegram messenger. Send messages, inline queries, payments, games, and mini apps. Simple HTTP-based API used globally.

name: WhatsApp Business API
description: Business messaging on WhatsApp via Meta's Cloud API. Templates, interactive messages, media, and catalog. Used by businesses worldwide for customer communication.

name: Twitch API
description: Live streaming platform API. Stream management, chat bots, channel points, subscriptions, and EventSub webhooks. Core platform for gaming and live content creators.

name: Linear
description: Modern project management for software teams. Issues, cycles, projects, and roadmaps. Official MCP server available. Known for speed and keyboard-first UX.

guide.auth_overview: Two auth methods: (1) OAuth 2.0 with PKCE for user-installed apps (preferred) and (2) Personal API Key (for personal / service-account use). Bearer token in Authorization header: `Authorization: {api_key}` — note the absence of the word "Bearer" for API keys; OAuth tokens use "Bearer {token}".
guide.setup_hint: 1. For personal scripts/agents: Settings > API > Personal API keys > Create key. 2. For multi-user integrations: create an OAuth application at Settings > API > OAuth applications. 3. The official Linear MCP server handles the full OAuth flow.

endpoints_sample.description: Get a single issue by ID or identifier
Search/list issues with filters (team, state, assignee)

agent_tips_sample: Linear is GraphQL-only — there is no REST API. Every call is POST /graphql.
Personal API keys use plain Authorization header WITHOUT the word "Bearer". OAuth tokens DO require "Bearer". This is a common source of 401 errors.
Always scope queries to a team (teamId) when searching issues — the workspace may have thousands.
guide.rate_limit: 1,500 GraphQL complexity points per hour per API key for personal tokens, 5,000 complexity/hour for OAuth apps. Each field/mutation has a complexity weight — check the X-Complexity and X-RateLimit-Remaining response headers.

name: Jira
description: Industry-standard issue tracking and project management by Atlassian. Scrum, Kanban, backlogs, and sprints. Comprehensive REST API and Connect framework.

name: monday.com
description: Work OS for teams. Customizable boards, automations, integrations, and dashboards. GraphQL API for managing items, boards, and workflows.

name: Figma
description: Collaborative interface design tool. Design, prototype, and handoff in the browser. REST API for file inspection, components, and comments. Dev Mode for developers.

name: Canva API
description: Design platform API for creating and editing visual content. Templates, brand kits, and asset management. Connect API for building integrations.

name: Miro
description: Online collaborative whiteboard platform. Boards, sticky notes, diagrams, and mind maps. REST API for board content management and automation.

name: Airtable
description: Low-code platform combining spreadsheets and databases. Views, automations, and interfaces. REST API for records, tables, and field management.

name: Coda
description: All-in-one document platform combining docs, spreadsheets, and apps. Packs for integrations. REST API for docs, tables, rows, and formulas.

name: Google Sheets API
description: Programmatic access to Google Sheets. Read, write, and format spreadsheet data. Widely used as a lightweight database and reporting tool by developers.

name: Calendly
description: Scheduling automation platform. Meeting booking, availability management, and calendar integrations. REST API for events, invitees, and scheduling links.

name: Loom
description: Video messaging platform for async communication. Record screen, camera, or both. REST API for video management, transcripts, and embeds.

name: Mailchimp
description: Email marketing and automation platform. Campaigns, audiences, templates, and analytics. Comprehensive REST API. Used by millions of businesses globally.

name: Resend
description: Modern email API for developers. Built on AWS SES with great DX. React Email for templates. Simple REST API focused on transactional email delivery.

guide.auth_overview: Bearer token authentication with a Resend API key (re_...). Keys are created per-project in the Resend dashboard and can be scoped to a single domain. Each key grants full send access to the domains it authorizes.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Sign up at https://resend.com and verify a sending domain (add DNS records: MX, TXT for SPF, DKIM). 2. Go to API Keys > Create API Key, scope to the verified domain. 3. Store in RESEND_API_KEY env var — the @resend/node SDK picks it up automatically.

endpoints_sample.description: Send a transactional email (HTML, text, or React component)

agent_tips_sample: Resend is optimized for developers — the API surface is small (emails, domains, audiences, batch) and well-typed. Prefer it over SendGrid when you don't need marketing features.
Domain verification requires DNS propagation — allow 5-15 minutes after adding records before retrying verification.
React Email templates are a first-class feature — you can pass a React component as the body in the official SDK.
guide.rate_limit: 10 requests/second per API key on the Free tier. Pro tier: 20/sec. 429 with Retry-After header. Batch endpoint counts as one request regardless of payload size (up to 100 emails).

name: Postmark
description: Transactional email service with industry-leading delivery speed. Templates, message streams, and detailed analytics. REST API with excellent deliverability.

guide.auth_overview: Token authentication via custom headers: `X-Postmark-Server-Token` for transactional operations (bound to a single Postmark Server), or `X-Postmark-Account-Token` for account-level operations (managing servers, domains). NOT Bearer auth.
guide.setup_hint: 1. In the Postmark dashboard, create a Server (one per environment — prod/staging). 2. Copy the Server API Token from the Server > API Tokens tab. 3. Verify a Sender Signature or Domain before sending. 4. Store tokens in POSTMARK_SERVER_TOKEN env var.

endpoints_sample.description: Send up to 500 emails in one request
Send using a stored template with merge variables

agent_tips_sample: Authentication is NOT Bearer — use `X-Postmark-Server-Token` header. Many generic HTTP clients will need custom header config.
All JSON field names are PascalCase (From, To, TextBody, MessageID) — a Postmark quirk from its .NET roots.
MessageStream field distinguishes transactional ("outbound") from broadcast ("broadcast") — mixing them violates Postmark TOS.
guide.rate_limit: No hard per-second rate limit — Postmark is optimized for high throughput. Large accounts can send millions/hour. 429 responses indicate abuse-detection triggers (e.g., unverified senders).

name: ConvertKit (Kit)
description: Creator-focused email marketing platform. Newsletters, automations, landing pages, and commerce. REST API for subscribers, broadcasts, and sequences.

name: PagerDuty
description: Incident management and on-call scheduling platform. Alerts, escalation policies, and incident response automation. REST API for services, incidents, and schedules.

name: Grafana Cloud
description: Observability platform for metrics, logs, and traces. Dashboards, alerting, and integrations with Prometheus, Loki, and Tempo. REST and HTTP APIs.

name: New Relic
description: Full-stack observability platform. APM, infrastructure monitoring, logs, browser, and mobile. NerdGraph (GraphQL) API and REST API for data and configuration.

name: Cloudflare
description: Internet infrastructure and security company. CDN, DNS, Workers, R2, D1, and security services. Official MCP server available for Workers and AI Gateway.

guide.auth_overview: API token (recommended) or API key + email. Tokens are scoped and more secure. Pass as Bearer token in Authorization header.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Go to dash.cloudflare.com/profile/api-tokens. 2. Create token with specific permissions (e.g., Zone:DNS:Edit). 3. Use Authorization: Bearer {token}. 4. Legacy: X-Auth-Email + X-Auth-Key headers (not recommended).

endpoints_sample.description: List DNS records for a zone

agent_tips_sample: Always check 'success' field in response — HTTP 200 doesn't mean success.
Use API tokens over global API key — tokens are scoped and revocable.
Zone ID is required for most operations — get it from GET /zones first.
guide.rate_limit: 1,200 req/5min globally. Some endpoints lower (e.g., Workers: 1,000/min).

name: Shopify
description: Leading global e-commerce platform. Storefronts, checkout, payments, and fulfillment. Admin API (REST and GraphQL) and Storefront API for headless commerce.

name: WooCommerce
description: Open-source e-commerce plugin for WordPress. Products, orders, coupons, and shipping. REST API for full store management. Powers millions of online stores globally.

name: Lemon Squeezy
description: Merchant of record platform for selling digital products and SaaS. Handles payments, taxes, and subscriptions globally. REST API for products, orders, and licenses.

name: Gumroad
description: デジタルコンテンツ・クリエイター向け販売プラットフォーム。電子書籍、音楽、ソフトウェア、コース販売。Simple REST API for products, sales, and subscribers. Popular with indie creators.

name: Pipedrive
description: Sales-focused CRM with visual pipeline management. Deals, contacts, activities, and automations. Comprehensive REST API for all CRM entities.

guide.auth_overview: API token or OAuth 2.0. API token from Settings > Personal preferences > API. Pass as api_token query parameter or OAuth Bearer.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Settings > Personal preferences > API > copy API token. 2. Append ?api_token={token} to all requests. 3. For OAuth: register app in Marketplace, implement auth code flow.
agent_tips_sample: API token in query param is simplest but less secure — use OAuth for production.
All responses have success boolean — check it, HTTP 200 doesn't guarantee success.
Custom fields use hashed keys (e.g., 'abc123_custom_field') — GET /dealFields to map names.
guide.rate_limit: 80 req/2sec (Essential), 160/2sec (Advanced), 240/2sec (Professional). X-RateLimit-* headers.

name: Stripe
description: Global payment processing platform. Payments, subscriptions, invoicing, and Connect. Official MCP server via Agent Toolkit. The standard for developer-first payments.

guide.auth_overview: API key as Bearer token. Secret key (sk_) for server-side, Publishable key (pk_) for client-side. Test mode keys start with sk_test_.
