guide.setup_hint: 1. Sign up at dashboard.stripe.com. 2. Get API keys from Developers > API keys. 3. Use sk_test_ keys for development. 4. Switch to sk_live_ for production.
agent_tips_sample: IMPORTANT: Request body uses form-encoded, NOT JSON (despite JSON responses).
Always use Payment Intents API over legacy Charges API.
Test card numbers: 4242424242424242 (success), 4000000000000002 (decline).
guide.rate_limit: 100 req/sec (read), 25 req/sec (write) in test mode. Live: 100 read, 100 write.

name: PayPal
description: Global online payment platform. Checkout, subscriptions, payouts, and invoicing. REST API v2 for payments and orders. Accepted in 200+ markets worldwide.

name: Paddle
description: Merchant of record for B2B SaaS. Handles payments, taxes, and subscriptions globally. Billing API for checkout, subscriptions, and pricing.

name: Wise (TransferWise) API
description: International money transfer platform. Multi-currency accounts, batch payments, and real-time exchange rates. REST API for transfers and account management.

name: Amazon S3
description: Object storage service by AWS. Industry standard for file storage, static hosting, and data lakes. REST API with comprehensive SDK support across all languages.

name: Cloudinary
description: Media management platform for images and videos. Upload, transform, optimize, and deliver media via CDN. REST API and SDKs for programmable media pipelines.

name: UploadThing
description: File upload service built for TypeScript developers. Simple integration with Next.js, SvelteKit, and other frameworks. Type-safe file routing and S3-backed storage.

name: AWS (Lambda / DynamoDB / RDS)
description: Amazon Web Services core compute and database services. Lambda for serverless functions, DynamoDB for NoSQL, RDS for managed relational databases. Comprehensive SDKs and REST APIs. Community MCP servers available for S3, Lambda, and DynamoDB.

guide.auth_overview: AWS Signature Version 4 (SigV4). Use IAM access key + secret key. AWS SDK handles signing automatically. Region-specific endpoints.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Create IAM user/role with Lambda permissions in AWS Console. 2. Generate access key + secret key. 3. Configure AWS SDK: aws configure or env vars AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID + AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY. 4. Set AWS_REGION.
agent_tips_sample: Always use AWS SDK instead of raw HTTP — SigV4 signing is complex.
For JP workloads, use ap-northeast-1 (Tokyo) region.
InvocationType: 'Event' for async, 'RequestResponse' for sync, 'DryRun' for validation.
guide.rate_limit: 1,000 concurrent executions (default soft limit). API calls: varies by action. Invoke: no hard limit but throttled by concurrency.

name: Google Cloud Platform
description: Google's cloud computing platform. Compute Engine, Cloud Functions, Cloud Run, Pub/Sub, Cloud Storage, and 100+ services. Extensive REST and gRPC APIs. Terraform and SDK support.

guide.auth_overview: OAuth 2.0 service account or user credentials. Service account JSON key recommended for server-to-server. Application Default Credentials (ADC) simplifies auth.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Create project in Google Cloud Console. 2. Enable required APIs. 3. Create service account + download JSON key. 4. Set GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=/path/to/key.json. 5. Use google-cloud SDK — ADC handles the rest.
agent_tips_sample: Always use official SDKs (@google-cloud/*) — they handle auth, retries, pagination.
Set GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS env var for service account auth.
For JP compliance, use asia-northeast1 (Tokyo) or asia-northeast2 (Osaka) regions.
guide.rate_limit: Per-API quotas. Default: 600 req/min for most APIs. Quotas page in Console for details.

name: Microsoft Azure
description: Microsoft's cloud platform. Azure Functions, Cosmos DB, Azure AI, App Service, and enterprise integration. REST APIs with OpenAPI specs. Strong enterprise adoption.

name: CircleCI
description: Continuous integration and delivery (CI/CD) platform. Pipeline orchestration, Docker-based builds, parallelism, test splitting. REST API v2 for pipeline management and insights.

guide.auth_overview: Personal API token as Bearer or in Circle-Token header. Generate at circleci.com > User Settings > Personal API Tokens.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Go to circleci.com > User Settings > Personal API Tokens. 2. Create token. 3. Use as 'Circle-Token' header or Bearer token.
agent_tips_sample: Project slug format: gh/{org}/{repo} for GitHub, bb/{org}/{repo} for Bitbucket.
Trigger pipeline with parameters for dynamic configuration.
Use Insights API for test and performance metrics.
guide.rate_limit: No published hard limit. Fair use policy.

name: GitHub Actions
description: GitHub's built-in CI/CD platform. Workflow automation with YAML configuration, marketplace of 20,000+ actions. Managed via GitHub REST API (workflow runs, artifacts, secrets).

guide.auth_overview: Personal access token or GitHub App token as Bearer. Fine-grained tokens recommended.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Settings > Developer settings > Personal access tokens (fine-grained). 2. Or create GitHub App for org-wide access.
agent_tips_sample: Include X-GitHub-Api-Version header for consistent behavior.
workflow_dispatch event allows manual/API triggering of workflows.
Artifacts are downloadable as zip — use Accept: application/vnd.github+json.
guide.rate_limit: 5,000 req/hour (authenticated). GitHub App: 15,000/hour per installation.

name: Heroku
description: Platform-as-a-service for building, running, and scaling apps. Git-based deploys, add-on marketplace, Heroku Postgres. Platform API for app and dyno management.

guide.auth_overview: API key or OAuth token as Bearer. Get API key at dashboard.heroku.com > Account > API Key.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Go to dashboard.heroku.com > Account Settings. 2. Scroll to API Key > Reveal. 3. Or use heroku auth:token from CLI.
agent_tips_sample: Include Accept: application/vnd.heroku+json; version=3 header.
Range-based pagination is unique to Heroku — use Range header.
App names are globally unique. Use /apps/{name} or /apps/{id}.
guide.rate_limit: 4,500 req/hour per OAuth token.

name: Turso
description: Edge-hosted distributed SQLite database built on libSQL. Low-latency reads from 30+ regions. REST API and native SDKs. Embedded replicas for local-first apps.

guide.auth_overview: API token as Bearer. Generate at turso.tech dashboard or turso auth api-tokens mint.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Install Turso CLI: curl -sSfL https://get.tur.so/install.sh | bash. 2. turso auth login. 3. turso auth api-tokens mint. 4. Use as Bearer token.
agent_tips_sample: Two APIs: Platform API (manage DBs) and libSQL protocol (query data).
Connect to DB via libsql:// URL with auth token — not the Platform API.
Embedded replicas: sync to local SQLite file for ultra-low latency reads.
guide.rate_limit: No published limit for Platform API. Database queries: plan-based.

name: Google Meet
description: Google's video conferencing platform integrated with Google Workspace. Meeting creation, calendar integration, recording management via Google Calendar and Admin APIs.

guide.auth_overview: OAuth 2.0 with Google Workspace. Enable Google Meet API in Cloud Console.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Enable Google Meet REST API in Cloud Console. 2. Create OAuth credentials. 3. Requires Google Workspace account (not personal Gmail).
agent_tips_sample: Requires Google Workspace — not available for personal Gmail accounts.
Meet API is relatively new (2024) — limited features vs Calendar API.
Creating a space returns a meetingUri for joining.
guide.rate_limit: Google Workspace API limits apply. Default: 600 req/min.

name: Mixpanel
description: Product analytics and customer data platform. Event tracking, funnel analysis, retention reports, cohort analysis. Behavioral data CDP. Ingestion API for events, Query API (JQL) for data export and analysis.

guide.auth_overview: Service account (username:secret) for query APIs. Project token for ingestion.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Create Service Account at mixpanel.com > Project Settings > Service Accounts. 2. Use username:secret as Basic auth for query APIs. 3. Use project token for /track endpoint.
agent_tips_sample: Two auth methods: project token for tracking, service account for querying.
Use /import for server-side event ingestion (more reliable than /track).
JQL (JavaScript Query Language) for complex custom queries.
guide.rate_limit: Ingestion: no published limit. Query: 60 req/hour for free, higher for paid.

name: Amplitude
description: Digital analytics and customer data platform for product intelligence. Behavioral analytics, experimentation, CDP. HTTP API for event ingestion, Cohort/Export APIs for data retrieval.

guide.auth_overview: API key + secret. Pass API key for ingestion, API key:secret for export/query.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Go to Amplitude > Settings > Projects > API Keys. 2. Copy API Key and Secret Key. 3. API Key for tracking, both for data export.
agent_tips_sample: API key goes in request body for ingestion, not header.
user_id OR device_id required per event — at least one.
Use Identify API to set user properties separately from events.
guide.rate_limit: Ingestion: 1,000 events/sec. Query: varies by endpoint.

name: Segment (Twilio)
description: Customer Data Platform. Collect, clean, and route user data to 400+ destinations. Track API for events, Profiles API for user data, Connections API for source/destination management.

guide.auth_overview: Write key as Basic auth username for tracking. Access token for Config API.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Create source in Segment workspace. 2. Copy Write Key for tracking. 3. For Config API: create access token at segment.com > Settings > Access Tokens.
agent_tips_sample: Write key as Basic auth username with empty password (like BambooHR pattern).
Use /batch for server-side — sends up to 500KB per request.
Segment routes data to 400+ destinations automatically.
guide.rate_limit: 500 req/sec (tracking). Config API: 10 req/sec.

name: PostHog
description: Open-source product analytics suite. Analytics, session replay, feature flags, A/B testing, surveys. Self-host or cloud. REST API and community MCP server available.

guide.auth_overview: Personal API key as Bearer token. Project API key for event capture.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Go to PostHog > Settings > Personal API Keys. 2. Create key. 3. Use as Bearer token for API queries. 4. Project API key for event capture.
agent_tips_sample: Two key types: Personal API key (queries), Project API key (capture).
Self-hostable — Docker setup for local development.
HogQL is PostHog's query language — SQL-like for custom analysis.
guide.rate_limit: No hard limit for Cloud. Self-hosted: depends on deployment.

name: Klaviyo
description: Email and SMS marketing automation for ecommerce. Flows, campaigns, segmentation, predictive analytics. REST API v3 for lists, profiles, campaigns, and event tracking.

guide.auth_overview: Private API key as Bearer token. Generate at klaviyo.com > Account > Settings > API Keys.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Go to Klaviyo > Account > Settings > API Keys. 2. Create Private API Key with needed scopes. 3. Set revision header to latest.
agent_tips_sample: Authorization format is 'Klaviyo-API-Key {key}' — NOT Bearer.
Must include 'revision' header (date string) for API versioning.
JSON:API format — data is nested in 'data' array with 'attributes'.
guide.rate_limit: 75 req/sec for most endpoints. Some: 10/sec. Retry-After header.

name: ActiveCampaign
description: Marketing automation and CRM platform. Email marketing, sales automation, messaging, machine learning-powered predictions. REST API v3 for contacts, deals, automations.

guide.auth_overview: API key in Api-Token header. Find at Settings > Developer.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Go to ActiveCampaign > Settings > Developer. 2. Copy API URL and Key. 3. API URL contains your account name.
agent_tips_sample: API URL is account-specific: {account}.api-us1.com.
Auth header is 'Api-Token' — NOT Authorization Bearer.
Contact sync: POST /contact/sync for create-or-update behavior.
guide.rate_limit: 5 req/sec per account.

name: Brevo (Sendinblue)
description: All-in-one marketing platform. Email, SMS, WhatsApp, chat, CRM. Transactional and marketing email APIs. REST API v3 for contacts, campaigns, and automations.

guide.auth_overview: API key in api-key header. Generate at app.brevo.com > SMTP & API.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Go to Brevo > SMTP & API > API Keys. 2. Generate v3 API key. 3. Pass in 'api-key' header.
agent_tips_sample: Auth header is 'api-key' — lowercase, NOT Authorization Bearer.
Covers email, SMS, WhatsApp, and chat from one API.
Transactional vs Marketing emails: different endpoints and limits.
guide.rate_limit: Depends on plan. Starter: 100 emails/hour. Free: 300 emails/day.

name: Customer.io
description: Messaging automation platform. Behavioral-triggered emails, push notifications, SMS, in-app messages. Track API for events, App API for campaigns and segments.

guide.auth_overview: Track API: site_id:api_key as Basic auth. App API: Bearer token from app.customer.io.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Go to customer.io > Settings > API Credentials. 2. Track API: use site_id and API key. 3. App API: create Bearer token for campaigns/segments.
agent_tips_sample: Two APIs: Track (behavioral data) and App (campaigns/segments).
Track API uses Basic auth (site_id:api_key), App API uses Bearer token.
Customer ID is your choice — use your internal user ID.
guide.rate_limit: Track API: 100 req/sec. App API: 10 req/sec.

name: QuickBooks Online
description: Intuit's cloud accounting platform. #1 SMB accounting software globally. Invoicing, expenses, payroll, tax. REST API v3 with OAuth 2.0. 750+ app integrations.

guide.auth_overview: OAuth 2.0. Register app at developer.intuit.com. Use OAuth Playground for testing.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Register at developer.intuit.com. 2. Create app for QuickBooks Online. 3. Use OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code flow. 4. Get realmId (company ID) during auth.
agent_tips_sample: realmId (company ID) is essential — obtained during OAuth flow.
Use SQL-like query language for flexible data retrieval.
Sandbox has pre-populated test data — great for development.
guide.rate_limit: 500 req/min per realmId. Concurrent: 10 requests. Throttle at 40% of limit.

name: Xero
description: Cloud accounting for small businesses. Popular in UK, AU, NZ. Invoicing, bank reconciliation, payroll, inventory. REST API with OAuth 2.0. 1000+ app marketplace.

guide.auth_overview: OAuth 2.0 with PKCE. Register at developer.xero.com. Multi-tenant: one token accesses multiple orgs.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Register at developer.xero.com. 2. Create app (PKCE for public clients). 3. Authorize and get tenant_id. 4. Include Xero-tenant-id header in all requests.
agent_tips_sample: Xero-tenant-id header is REQUIRED on every API call.
Use 'where' parameter with OData-like filtering for queries.
Demo company available for testing — no real data needed.
guide.rate_limit: 60 req/min per tenant. Daily: 5,000 API calls. App limit: 10,000/day across all tenants.

name: FreshBooks
description: Cloud accounting built for freelancers and service businesses. Time tracking, invoicing, expenses, proposals. REST API for invoices, clients, time entries.

guide.auth_overview: OAuth 2.0. Register at freshbooks.com/developers. Get account_id from /auth/api/v1/users/me.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Register at freshbooks.com/developers. 2. Create OAuth app. 3. Get account_id from /auth/api/v1/users/me after auth.
agent_tips_sample: account_id is mandatory in URL — get it first from /auth/api/v1/users/me.
Amounts are strings with 2 decimal places ("100.00").
Use ?include[]=lines to embed invoice line items in response.
guide.rate_limit: 20 req/sec per account. HTTP 429 with retry-after header.

name: Brex
description: Corporate card and spend management for startups and enterprises. Real-time expense tracking, budgets, bill pay. REST API for transactions, users, and card management.

guide.auth_overview: API token as Bearer. Generate at dashboard.brex.com > Developer > API tokens.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Log into dashboard.brex.com. 2. Go to Developer > API. 3. Create token with needed scopes.
agent_tips_sample: Amounts in cents (integer). 10000 = $100.00.
Idempotency-Key header recommended for POST/PUT requests.
Transaction categories are auto-classified by Brex.
guide.rate_limit: 1,000 req/min. HTTP 429 with retry-after.

name: Ramp
description: Corporate card and finance automation platform. Expense management, bill pay, procurement, accounting integrations. REST API for transactions and reimbursements.

guide.auth_overview: OAuth 2.0 client credentials. Register at ramp.com/developer.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Apply at ramp.com/developer. 2. Get client_id and client_secret. 3. Use client_credentials grant.
agent_tips_sample: Client credentials flow — no user interaction needed.
Transactions include merchant, category, and receipt data.
Amounts in cents. Negative amounts = credits/refunds.
guide.rate_limit: 100 req/min.

name: Mercury
description: Banking for startups. Business checking/savings, treasury, venture debt, corporate cards. API for transactions, accounts, and payment initiation.

guide.auth_overview: API token as Bearer. Generate at app.mercury.com > Settings > API.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Log into Mercury dashboard. 2. Settings > Developers > API Token. 3. Use as Bearer token.
agent_tips_sample: Amounts in cents (integer). 10000 = $100.00.
Payment creation requires approval — may not be instant.
Read-only token available for analytics without transfer risk.
guide.rate_limit: 60 req/min. HTTP 429.

name: Workday
description: Enterprise HCM and financial management platform. HR, payroll, talent management, workforce planning. REST and SOAP APIs. Dominant in enterprise HR globally.

guide.auth_overview: OAuth 2.0 with tenant-specific URLs. Requires Workday admin to register API client.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Workday admin creates API Client in Workday Studio. 2. Register redirect URI. 3. Assign ISSG permissions. 4. Tenant URL format: {company}.workday.com.

endpoints_sample.description: Query data using WQL (Workday Query Language)

agent_tips_sample: Tenant URL is company-specific — no universal base URL.
WQL (Workday Query Language) is powerful for custom data queries.
ISSG permissions are granular — admin must configure per integration.
guide.rate_limit: Tenant-specific. Typically 100 req/min for standard integrations.

name: BambooHR
description: HR software for small and medium businesses. Employee records, time-off tracking, onboarding, performance reviews. REST API for employee data and time tracking.

guide.auth_overview: API key with Basic auth. Key as username, 'x' as password.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Go to BambooHR > Account > API Keys. 2. Generate key. 3. Use as username in Basic auth with password 'x'.
agent_tips_sample: Subdomain is company-specific (e.g., mycompany.bamboohr.com → subdomain='mycompany').
Basic auth: API key as username, literal 'x' as password.
Employee fields are configurable — use GET /meta/fields for available fields.
guide.rate_limit: No published rate limit but throttled on abuse. Be reasonable with polling.

name: Gusto
description: Payroll, benefits, and HR platform for US small businesses. Full-service payroll, health insurance, 401(k). REST API for payroll, employees, and company management.

guide.auth_overview: OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code. Register at dev.gusto.com. Partner access required.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Apply at dev.gusto.com for partner access. 2. Create OAuth app. 3. Use demo company for testing.
agent_tips_sample: Partner application required — not self-serve API access.
Demo company available at gusto-demo.com for testing.
Payroll calculation is a multi-step process: create → calculate → submit.
guide.rate_limit: 60 req/min per token.

name: Rippling
description: Unified workforce platform. HR, IT, payroll, benefits, device management in one system. REST API. Fast-growing platform connecting HR and IT management.

guide.auth_overview: OAuth 2.0. Register at developer.rippling.com. Bearer token access.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Apply at developer.rippling.com. 2. Create app. 3. OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow.
agent_tips_sample: Rippling is a unified platform (HR + IT + Finance) — API spans multiple domains.
Employee data may include device management and app provisioning info.
Webhooks available for employee lifecycle events (hire, terminate, etc.).
guide.rate_limit: 100 req/min per app.

name: Deel
description: Global payroll and compliance platform. Hire and pay contractors and employees in 150+ countries. REST API for contracts, invoices, and payments.

guide.auth_overview: API token as Bearer. Generate at app.deel.com > Integrations > API.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Go to Deel dashboard > Developer > API. 2. Generate API token. 3. Use as Bearer token.
agent_tips_sample: Deel handles both employees (EOR) and contractors — different contract types.
Currency is per-contract — multi-currency by design.
Demo environment available for testing without real payments.
guide.rate_limit: 100 req/min.

name: Greenhouse
description: Structured hiring platform. ATS, interview scheduling, scorecards, offer management. Harvest API for candidates and jobs, Ingestion API for job board integration.

guide.auth_overview: Basic auth with API key. Key as username, empty password. Generate at Greenhouse > Configure > Dev Center.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Go to Greenhouse > Configure > Dev Center > API Credential Management. 2. Create Harvest API key. 3. Use as username in Basic auth (empty password).
agent_tips_sample: Harvest API for data access, Job Board API for public job listings.
Basic auth: API key as username, empty string as password.
Per-page max is 500 — use for bulk data retrieval.
guide.rate_limit: 50 req/10 sec. HTTP 429 with Retry-After.

name: Help Scout
description: Customer support platform with shared inbox, knowledge base, and live chat. REST API (Mailbox 2.0) for conversations, customers, and reports.

guide.auth_overview: OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code. Register at developer.helpscout.com. Or use API key with Basic auth.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Go to developer.helpscout.com > My Apps. 2. Create app with OAuth. 3. Or generate API key for basic auth (simpler).
agent_tips_sample: HAL+JSON format — links and embedded resources in _embedded.
Conversation threads: GET /conversations/{id}/threads for messages.
Tags, custom fields, and workflows are configurable per mailbox.
guide.rate_limit: 400 req/min per app. HTTP 429.

name: Front
description: Customer operations platform. Shared inbox for email, SMS, social. Workflow automation, analytics. REST API for conversations, contacts, and message operations.

guide.auth_overview: API token as Bearer. Generate at app.frontapp.com > Settings > API & integrations.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Go to Front > Settings > Developers > API tokens. 2. Create token. 3. Use as Bearer token.
agent_tips_sample: Conversations unify email, SMS, chat, and social into one thread.
Use q[] query params for filtering (status, tags, assignees).
Shared inboxes are the core unit — list them first.
guide.rate_limit: Starter: 50 req/min. Growth: 100/min. Scale: 200/min.

name: ServiceNow
description: Enterprise IT service management and digital workflow platform. ITSM, ITOM, HRSD. REST API (Table API, Scripted REST) for incident, change, and request management.

guide.auth_overview: OAuth 2.0 or Basic auth. Instance-specific URL. Admin creates OAuth app in System OAuth > Application Registry.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Admin registers OAuth app in ServiceNow instance. 2. Use client credentials or authorization code flow. 3. Or use Basic auth (username:password) for simple access.
agent_tips_sample: Instance URL is company-specific: {company}.service-now.com.
Table API is the most common — almost everything is a table.
sysparm_query uses encoded query format (e.g., priority=1^state=2).
guide.rate_limit: Instance-specific. Default: no hard limit but governed by instance performance.

name: Gorgias
description: Ecommerce-focused helpdesk. Deep Shopify/BigCommerce integration. Automate responses with macros and rules. REST API for tickets, customers, and integrations.

guide.auth_overview: API key + email as Basic auth. Generate at Settings > REST API in Gorgias.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Go to Gorgias > Settings > REST API. 2. Create API key. 3. Use email:api_key as Basic auth.
agent_tips_sample: Subdomain is your Gorgias account name.
Basic auth: email as username, API key as password.
Deep Shopify/BigCommerce integration — tickets have order context.
guide.rate_limit: 2 req/sec per API key.

name: X (Twitter) API
description: Social media platform API. Post tweets, read timelines, manage lists, search. API v2 with OAuth 2.0. Essential for social monitoring and engagement automation.

guide.auth_overview: OAuth 2.0 with PKCE for user context. OAuth 1.0a also supported. App-only: Bearer token.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Apply at developer.x.com. 2. Create project and app. 3. Get Bearer token for app-only access. 4. Use OAuth 2.0 PKCE for user-context actions.
agent_tips_sample: Free tier is very limited — 1,500 tweets/month read.
OAuth 2.0 PKCE required for posting (user context).
Use 'tweet.fields' and 'expansions' params to get full data.
guide.rate_limit: Free: 1,500 tweets read/month, 50 write/month. Basic ($100/mo): 10K read. Pro ($5K/mo): 1M read.

name: LinkedIn API
description: Professional network API. Share posts, manage company pages, Marketing API for ads, Community Management API. OAuth 2.0. Key B2B marketing channel.

guide.auth_overview: OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code. Register at linkedin.com/developers. 3-legged auth for user data.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Create app at linkedin.com/developers. 2. Add products (Share on LinkedIn, Marketing APIs). 3. OAuth 2.0 with redirect URI.
agent_tips_sample: Product approval needed for most APIs — not instant access.
Marketing APIs require separate approval and higher-tier partnership.
Access tokens expire in 60 days — refresh tokens last 365 days.
guide.rate_limit: 100 req/day for most endpoints. Application-level daily limits.

name: YouTube API
description: Video platform API by Google. Upload videos, manage playlists, read analytics, live streaming. Data API v3 and Analytics API. Largest video platform globally.

guide.auth_overview: API key for public data. OAuth 2.0 for user data (uploads, playlists). Enable YouTube Data API v3 in Google Cloud Console.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Enable YouTube Data API v3 in Google Cloud Console. 2. Create API key for read-only public data. 3. Create OAuth credentials for user operations.
agent_tips_sample: Quota is unit-based, NOT request-based. Search costs 100 units!
Use 'part' parameter wisely — each part (snippet, statistics, contentDetails) adds quota cost.
API key works for all public data — no OAuth needed for reading.
guide.rate_limit: 10,000 quota units/day. Each endpoint costs different units (search=100, list=1).

name: Buffer
description: SNS投稿管理プラットフォーム。Twitter, LinkedIn, Instagram, Facebook, TikTok への投稿スケジュール管理。REST API for publishing, analytics, and channel management.

guide.auth_overview: OAuth 2.0 or personal access token. Create at buffer.com/developers/api.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Register at buffer.com/developers/apps. 2. Create app. 3. Or use personal access token for quick start.
agent_tips_sample: Request body is form-encoded, NOT JSON.
Profile = connected social account. List profiles first to get IDs.
Schedule: set 'scheduled_at' for specific time, or use 'now' for immediate.
guide.rate_limit: No published rate limit. Fair use policy.

name: Brave Search
description: Privacy-focused search engine with independent index. Web search, news, and local results. Official MCP server by Anthropic. Top choice for AI agent web search.

guide.auth_overview: API key in X-Subscription-Token header. Get free key at brave.com/search/api/.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Go to brave.com/search/api. 2. Sign up and get API key (free tier: 2,000 queries/month). 3. Pass as X-Subscription-Token header.
agent_tips_sample: Official MCP server available: npx @anthropic/brave-search-mcp.
Free tier is generous (2K/month) — great for agent web search.
Use 'country' and 'search_lang' params for localized results.
guide.rate_limit: Free: 1 req/sec, 2,000/month. Paid: 20 req/sec. HTTP 429 on limit.

name: Tavily
description: AI-optimized search API built for LLMs and AI agents. Returns clean, structured search results. Official MCP server. Designed specifically for agentic workflows.

guide.auth_overview: API key in request body or Authorization header. Get key at app.tavily.com.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Sign up at app.tavily.com. 2. Get API key from dashboard. 3. Pass as 'api_key' in POST body or Bearer token.
agent_tips_sample: Designed for AI agents — returns clean, structured results optimized for LLMs.
Use search_depth='advanced' for deeper research (uses more credits).
include_answer=true returns an AI-generated summary with the results.
guide.rate_limit: Free: 1,000 searches/month. Paid: higher limits.

name: Playwright MCP
description: Browser automation framework by Microsoft with official MCP server. Navigate pages, fill forms, click elements, extract data. Key tool for AI agent web interaction.

name: Firecrawl
description: Web scraping API optimized for LLMs. Crawl websites and return clean markdown. Official MCP server. Handles JavaScript rendering, anti-bot bypass, and structured extraction.

guide.auth_overview: API key as Bearer token. Get key at firecrawl.dev.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Sign up at firecrawl.dev. 2. Get API key from dashboard. 3. Use as Bearer token in Authorization header.

endpoints_sample.description: Scrape a single URL to markdown/structured data

agent_tips_sample: Returns clean markdown — perfect for feeding into LLMs.
Use formats=['markdown','html'] to get both versions.
Crawl is asynchronous — returns job_id, poll for completion.
guide.rate_limit: Free: 500 credits/month. Each scrape = 1 credit, crawl pages = 1 credit each.

name: Google AI (Gemini)
description: Google's AI platform. Gemini models (Pro, Ultra, Flash), Vertex AI, embeddings, multimodal capabilities. REST API and SDKs. Competitive with OpenAI and Anthropic.

guide.auth_overview: API key authentication. Generate at aistudio.google.com or Google Cloud Console. Pass as ?key= query parameter or x-goog-api-key header.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Go to aistudio.google.com > Get API Key. 2. Or create in Google Cloud Console > APIs & Services > Credentials. 3. Enable Generative Language API.
agent_tips_sample: Use AI Studio (aistudio.google.com) for quick prototyping before coding.
Gemini 2.0 Flash is best for speed, Gemini 2.5 Pro for quality.
Multimodal: pass image as inline_data with base64 or file URI.
guide.rate_limit: Free tier: 15 req/min for Gemini Pro, 2 req/min for Gemini Ultra. Paid: 360 req/min. 1,500 req/day free.

name: Cohere
description: Enterprise AI platform. Command models, Embed for search, Rerank for relevance. Strong RAG capabilities. REST API with enterprise-grade security and deployment options.

guide.auth_overview: API key (Bearer token). Generate at dashboard.cohere.com/api-keys. Free trial key available.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Sign up at cohere.com. 2. Go to Dashboard > API Keys. 3. Copy key and use as Bearer token.
agent_tips_sample: Cohere excels at RAG — use /rerank after vector search for better results.
Embed v3 models support multiple input_types: search_document, search_query, classification.
For RAG: embed docs with input_type='search_document', queries with 'search_query'.
guide.rate_limit: Trial: 20 req/min. Production: 10,000 req/min. Rate limits in response headers.

name: Mistral AI
description: European AI lab. Mistral, Mixtral, and Codestral models. Function calling, JSON mode, vision. REST API compatible with OpenAI format. Strong open-weight model lineup.

guide.auth_overview: API key as Bearer token. Generate at console.mistral.ai. OpenAI SDK compatible.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Sign up at console.mistral.ai. 2. Generate API key. 3. Use with OpenAI SDK (change base_url) or Mistral SDK.
agent_tips_sample: OpenAI SDK compatible — change base_url to https://api.mistral.ai/v1.
Mistral Large for quality, Mistral Small for speed, Codestral for code.
FIM (fill-in-middle) endpoint is unique to Mistral — great for code infilling.
guide.rate_limit: Free tier: 1 req/sec, 500K tokens/month. Paid plans: higher. Check X-RateLimit headers.

name: Groq
description: Ultra-fast LLM inference on custom LPU hardware. Fastest token generation speeds. OpenAI-compatible API. Supports Llama, Mistral, Gemma models.

guide.auth_overview: API key authentication. Generate key at console.groq.com. Pass as Bearer token in Authorization header. OpenAI SDK compatible — just change base_url.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Sign up at console.groq.com. 2. Create API key in dashboard. 3. Use OpenAI SDK with base_url='https://api.groq.com/openai/v1'. 4. Or use curl with Authorization: Bearer {key}.
agent_tips_sample: OpenAI SDK drop-in: just set base_url to https://api.groq.com/openai/v1.
Fastest inference for open models — Llama 3, Mixtral, Gemma available.
Use 'llama-3.3-70b-versatile' for best quality, 'llama-3.1-8b-instant' for speed.
guide.rate_limit: Free tier: 30 req/min, 14,400 req/day. Paid: higher limits. Model-specific token limits (e.g., Llama 3.1 8B: 6,000 tokens/min free).

name: Perplexity
description: AI-powered search and answer engine. Sonar models with real-time web search built in. REST API (OpenAI-compatible) returns answers with citations. Used for research agents.

guide.auth_overview: API key as Bearer token. Generate at perplexity.ai/settings/api.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Sign up at perplexity.ai. 2. Go to Settings > API. 3. Generate key. 4. Requires payment method for API access.
agent_tips_sample: Perplexity is search-augmented — responses include citations from the web.
Use 'sonar-pro' for best quality with citations, 'sonar' for speed.
Responses include 'citations' array with source URLs.
guide.rate_limit: Varies by plan. Free: very limited. Pro: 50 req/min. Check response headers.

name: ElevenLabs
description: AI voice synthesis and cloning platform. Text-to-speech, voice cloning, dubbing, sound effects. REST API and official MCP server. Leading voice AI provider.

guide.auth_overview: API key in xi-api-key header. Generate at elevenlabs.io/app/settings/api-keys.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Sign up at elevenlabs.io. 2. Go to Profile > API Keys. 3. Pass key as xi-api-key header.

endpoints_sample.description: Generate a new voice from description

agent_tips_sample: Response is binary audio (mpeg) — save to file or stream directly.
Use eleven_multilingual_v2 model for non-English languages including Japanese.
Voice cloning requires Professional plan. Instant cloning needs ~1 min of audio.
guide.rate_limit: Free: 10K chars/month. Starter: 30K chars/month. Concurrent request limits vary by plan.

name: Qdrant
description: High-performance vector database. Similarity search, filtering, payload indexing. REST and gRPC APIs. Official MCP server. Open-source with managed cloud offering.

guide.auth_overview: API key authentication via Api-Key header (Qdrant Cloud) or open access (self-hosted by default, secure with a reverse proxy). Qdrant Cloud clusters expose HTTPS endpoints per cluster. Official MCP server: npx @qdrant/mcp-server.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Qdrant Cloud: create a free cluster at cloud.qdrant.io, generate an API key from the cluster dashboard. 2. Self-host: docker run -p 6333:6333 qdrant/qdrant (no auth by default — add API key via QDRANT__SERVICE__API_KEY env var). 3. MCP: npx @qdrant/mcp-server --url https://xyz.qdrant.io --api-key ***

endpoints_sample.description: Create a collection with vector size, distance metric (Cosine/Dot/Euclid), and optional quantization
Get collection info (vector count, status, config)
Upsert points (id, vector, payload). Supports batch via the 'batch' variant.

agent_tips_sample: Vector size MUST match the embedding model (1536 for text-embedding-3-small, 3072 for text-embedding-3-large, 768 for nomic-embed-text). Mismatched sizes produce errors on upsert.
Use Cosine distance for normalized embeddings (most OpenAI/Anthropic models) and Dot for raw inner-product semantics.
Payload filters combine with vector search — use them for tenant isolation, time filtering, or source restriction without re-indexing.
guide.rate_limit: No documented hard rate limit on self-hosted. Qdrant Cloud: depends on cluster tier — free tier ~100 req/s, paid tiers scale with cluster size. gRPC is preferred for bulk upserts (~5x faster than REST).

name: Chroma
description: Open-source embedding database for AI applications. Simple API for storing and querying embeddings. Python and JS clients. Popular for RAG prototyping.

guide.auth_overview: Chroma supports static API token authentication (Authorization header with `Bearer {token}`) and basic auth. Self-hosted deployments can be configured via env vars: `CHROMA_SERVER_AUTHN_CREDENTIALS` and `CHROMA_SERVER_AUTHN_PROVIDER`. Local/dev usage often runs with no auth.
guide.setup_hint: 1. For local dev: `pip install chromadb` and run `chroma run --path ./data` — no auth by default. 2. For production: set CHROMA_SERVER_AUTHN_PROVIDER=token and CHROMA_SERVER_AUTHN_CREDENTIALS. 3. The `chroma-mcp` package wraps the HTTP client and handles auth via env vars.
agent_tips_sample: Chroma's v2 API changed collection routing — use /tenants/{tenant}/databases/{db}/collections, not the v1 /collections path.
If you configure an embedding function at collection creation, `add` accepts raw `documents` and Chroma embeds them automatically.
The `where` filter supports MongoDB-style operators ($and, $or, $in, $ne) for metadata filtering.
guide.rate_limit: No built-in rate limit (self-hosted). Throughput bound by hardware and embedding model latency.

name: Langfuse
description: Open-source LLM observability and evaluation platform. Tracing, prompt management, scoring, datasets. REST API and official MCP server. Essential for LLM ops.

guide.auth_overview: Basic auth with public_key:secret_key. Generate keys at cloud.langfuse.com project settings.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Create project at cloud.langfuse.com. 2. Go to Settings > API Keys. 3. Use public_key as username, secret_key as password in Basic auth.
agent_tips_sample: Use Python/JS SDK for automatic tracing — much easier than raw API.
Batch ingestion endpoint accepts up to 1000 events per request.
Self-hosting available: docker compose up for local development.
guide.rate_limit: Cloud: 1,000 req/min. Self-hosted: unlimited. Batch ingestion recommended.

name: BigCommerce
description: Enterprise ecommerce platform. Headless commerce, multi-channel selling, B2B support. REST and GraphQL APIs. Strong API-first architecture for composable commerce.

guide.auth_overview: API token (X-Auth-Token header) + Client ID (X-Auth-Client header). Create at devtools.bigcommerce.com.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Go to BigCommerce admin > Settings > API > API Accounts. 2. Create V2/V3 API Account. 3. Use store_hash from API path, X-Auth-Token and X-Auth-Client headers.
agent_tips_sample: Two headers required: X-Auth-Token AND X-Auth-Client.
store_hash is in the API URL — unique per store.
Orders use v2 API: /stores/{hash}/v2/orders.
guide.rate_limit: Standard: 4 req/sec. Plus: 7/sec. Pro/Enterprise: 7-12/sec.

name: Klarna
description: Buy-now-pay-later and payment platform. Installment payments, pay later, financing. Payments API, Order Management API. Major BNPL provider globally.

guide.auth_overview: API key (username:password) as Basic auth. Obtain from Klarna Merchant Portal.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Log into Klarna Merchant Portal. 2. Go to Settings > API credentials. 3. Use as Basic auth (username:password).
agent_tips_sample: Amounts in minor units (cents): 10000 = $100.00.
Playground (sandbox) uses different base URL — api.playground.klarna.com.
Order flow: create session → authorize → capture.
guide.rate_limit: Not published. Contact Klarna for high-volume needs.

name: Adyen
description: Enterprise payment platform. Unified commerce across online, in-store, and mobile. 250+ payment methods, risk management. REST API for payments and payouts.

guide.auth_overview: API key in X-API-Key header. Generate in Adyen Customer Area > Developers > API credentials.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Log into Adyen Customer Area. 2. Go to Developers > API credentials. 3. Generate API key. 4. Use test prefix URL for sandbox.
agent_tips_sample: Amounts are in minor units: {value:10000, currency:'USD'} = $100.00.
Test URL: checkout-test.adyen.com. Live URL: checkout-live.adyen.com/{prefix}.
250+ payment methods supported — use /paymentMethods to discover available ones.
guide.rate_limit: Test: 100 req/sec. Live: depends on contract.

name: PostgreSQL MCP
description: World's most advanced open-source relational database. Official MCP server by Anthropic for read-only queries. AI agents can inspect schemas and query data directly.

guide.auth_overview: Connection string with PostgreSQL credentials (user/password/host/db). The official MCP server by Anthropic (npx @anthropic/postgres-mcp) is strictly read-only and accepts a PostgreSQL connection URL as its argument. Authentication is delegated to Postgres itself (password, scram, client cert, or peer).
guide.setup_hint: 1. Create a read-only Postgres role: CREATE ROLE agent_ro LOGIN PASSWORD '...'; GRANT pg_read_all_data TO agent_ro; 2. Connection string: postgres://agent_ro:***@host:5432/db. 3. Run: npx @anthropic/postgres-mcp 'postgres://...' — the server enforces read-only at the protocol level.

endpoints_sample.description: Execute a read-only SQL query and return rows. Blocks any statement that isn't SELECT/WITH.
List all schemas in the database (excluding system schemas)
List tables in a given schema with row count estimates

agent_tips_sample: The official MCP server is READ-ONLY by design — attempting INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE returns an error at the MCP layer before hitting Postgres.
Always set statement_timeout in the connection string (e.g., ?options=-c%20statement_timeout%3D30s) to prevent runaway queries from blocking other agents.
Use pg_stat_user_tables and pg_stat_statements for schema and performance inspection — these are the agent's main windows into DB health.
guide.rate_limit: No MCP-level rate limit. Postgres-side: respect statement_timeout, max_connections, and any pg_stat_statements throttling. Agents should honor statement_timeout (recommended 30s) to prevent runaway queries.

name: MySQL
description: Popular open-source relational database by Oracle. Community MCP server available for schema inspection and queries. Most widely deployed open-source database.

name: Redis
description: In-memory data store for caching, queuing, and real-time applications. Key-value, streams, pub/sub, search. Community MCP server. Redis Cloud for managed service.

guide.base_url: redis:// or rediss:// (TLS) — binary RESP protocol, not HTTP
guide.auth_overview: Redis uses the AUTH command at connection time. Modern Redis (6+) supports ACL users with fine-grained command/key permissions: `AUTH {username} {password}`. Legacy single-password mode uses `AUTH {password}` only. TLS is recommended for any network-exposed deployment.
guide.setup_hint: 1. In redis.conf, set `requirepass` (legacy) or configure ACL users via `ACL SETUSER`. 2. For managed Redis (Upstash, AWS ElastiCache, Redis Cloud) the credentials are provided in the dashboard. 3. The Redis MCP server accepts a connection string: `redis://user:pass@host:port/db`.

endpoints_sample.description: Set a string value with optional TTL

agent_tips_sample: NEVER use KEYS in production — it's O(n) and blocks the server. Use SCAN with a MATCH pattern instead.
Set TTLs on everything by default (SET with EX, EXPIRE) — unbounded growth is the #1 Redis incident.
For atomic multi-step operations, use MULTI/EXEC (transactions) or Lua scripts via EVAL.
guide.rate_limit: No protocol-level rate limit — throughput is bound by CPU, network, and client count. Managed services (Upstash, ElastiCache) may throttle at the network layer. Watch `INFO clients` and `slowlog get` for overload signs.

name: Framer
description: Modern web design and publishing platform. Visual design to production website. Component-based, CMS, localization. REST API for sites and CMS content management.

guide.auth_overview: API key as Bearer token. Generate at framer.com > Account Settings > API.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Go to framer.com account settings. 2. Generate API token. 3. Use as Bearer token.
agent_tips_sample: API is primarily for CMS content management, not visual design.
Design changes are made in Framer editor, not via API.
CMS collections map to content types (blog posts, products, etc.).
guide.rate_limit: Not published. Fair use.

name: Webflow
description: Visual web development platform. Design, build, and launch responsive websites. CMS, ecommerce, memberships. REST API for CMS items, forms, and site management.

guide.auth_overview: OAuth 2.0 or site API token as Bearer. Create at webflow.com/dashboard/account/integrations.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Go to Site Settings > Integrations > API Access. 2. Generate Site API token. 3. Or create OAuth app at webflow.com/developers.
agent_tips_sample: v2 API is current — v1 deprecated.
CMS items must match collection schema — check fields first.
Publish after CMS changes: POST /sites/{id}/publish.
guide.rate_limit: 60 req/min per site.

name: Typeform
description: Conversational form and survey platform. Interactive forms, quizzes, surveys with branching logic. REST API for form creation, responses, and webhooks.

guide.auth_overview: Personal access token as Bearer. Generate at admin.typeform.com > Account > Personal tokens.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Go to admin.typeform.com > Account > Personal tokens. 2. Create token with needed scopes. 3. Or use OAuth for app integration.
agent_tips_sample: Form structure uses 'fields' array with types (short_text, multiple_choice, etc.).
Responses are keyed by field.ref — set refs during form creation for stable keys.
Webhook integration: POST /forms/{id}/webhooks for real-time responses.
guide.rate_limit: 2 req/sec. Higher limits on paid plans.

name: Tally
description: Free form builder with powerful features. No-code forms with calculations, conditional logic, file uploads. Webhooks and Zapier integration. API for responses.

guide.auth_overview: No traditional API. Uses webhooks for form submissions. Zapier/Make integrations available.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Create form at tally.so. 2. Go to form > Integrations > Webhooks. 3. Set webhook URL for form submissions. 4. Or use Zapier/Make for automation.
agent_tips_sample: No REST API for reading forms — webhook-only for submissions.
Zapier and Make integrations are the primary automation path.
Webhook payload includes all field values with labels.
guide.rate_limit: N/A — submissions are pushed via webhooks.

name: Snowflake
description: Cloud data platform for data warehousing, data lakes, and data sharing. SQL-based analytics at scale. REST API (Snowpipe, Snowpark). Cortex AI for LLM integration.

name: BigQuery
description: Google's serverless data warehouse. Petabyte-scale analytics with SQL. Streaming inserts, ML integration (BigQuery ML), BI Engine. REST API and client libraries.

guide.auth_overview: OAuth 2.0 or service account. Enable BigQuery API in Google Cloud Console.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Enable BigQuery API in Cloud Console. 2. Create service account with BigQuery roles. 3. Download JSON key file. 4. Or use Application Default Credentials.
agent_tips_sample: Free tier: 1TB query processing/month, 10GB storage.
Always set useLegacySql:false for standard SQL.
Use client libraries (@google-cloud/bigquery) over raw REST.
guide.rate_limit: Concurrent queries: 100 per project. API: 100 req/sec.

name: Databricks
description: Unified analytics platform built on Apache Spark. Data engineering, ML, SQL analytics, lakehouse architecture. REST API for jobs, clusters, notebooks, and Unity Catalog.

guide.auth_overview: Personal access token as Bearer. Generate in Databricks workspace > User Settings > Developer.
guide.setup_hint: 1. Log into Databricks workspace. 2. User Settings > Developer > Access Tokens. 3. Generate token. 4. Use workspace URL from your deployment.
agent_tips_sample: Workspace URL is deployment-specific: {workspace}.cloud.databricks.com.
SQL Statements API requires a SQL Warehouse — check warehouse_id.
Unity Catalog for data governance — 3-level namespace: catalog.schema.table.
guide.rate_limit: 30 req/sec per workspace. Rate-limited endpoints return 429.

name: Make (Integromat)
description: Visual automation platform connecting 1,800+ apps. Build complex workflows with branching, error handling, and data transformation. Formerly Integromat.

name: Japan Post (日本郵便)
description: Japan Post shipping and tracking API. Generate shipping labels, track parcels, and calculate shipping rates for domestic and international delivery.

name: Otter.ai
description: AI meeting transcription and note-taking. Real-time transcription, automated summaries, and action items from meetings. Integrates with Zoom, Teams, Google Meet.

name: Zoom
description: ビデオ会議・ウェビナー・電話のプラットフォーム。オンラインミーティング、画面共有、録画、チャット。REST API + WebSocket for meetings, users, recordings, reports. 日本企業に広く普及。

name: Ahrefs
description: SEO分析・競合調査プラットフォーム。バックリンク分析、キーワードリサーチ、サイト監査、ランキング追跡。REST API for backlinks, keywords, site audit, SERP data.

name: Sake Navi MCP
description: AIソムリエによる日本酒推薦MCP。3,167銘柄・47都道府県をカバー。銘柄検索、料理ペアリング（SSI 4タイプ分類×3原理）、味わいプロフィール（6軸）、地域探索。唎酒師の知識体系をアルゴリズム化。Search 3,167 Japanese sake brands, get food pairing recommendations using sommelier logic, explore regional specialties.

name: Card Navi MCP
description: 日本のクレジットカード最適化MCP。189枚のカード・553の店舗別還元率ルール。店舗指定で最もお得なカードを即回答。カード比較、詳細スペック、シナジー分析、ポイント交換ルート。Credit card optimizer for 189 Japanese cards with store-specific reward rate recommendations and side-by-side comparison.
